alignments.rationale: Both statements assert the AAE's effectiveness at generating new protein sequences; wording is slightly different but the claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clustering results on the encoded sequences from the latent space computed by AAEs display a high level of homogeneity regarding the protein sequence functions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clustering results on the encoded sequences from the latent space computed by AAEs display a high level of homogeneity regarding the protein sequence functions.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this clustering result directly, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study reports and analyzes two sampling strategies based on latent space interpolation and latent space arithmetic to generate intermediate protein sequences sharing sequential and functional properties.
alignments.pub_claim_text: 3D structure models computed using generated sequences indicate the ability of latent space arithmetic to successfully transfer protein sequence properties linked to function between different sub-families.
alignments.rationale: The preprint described reporting and analysis of strategies; the publication presents an additional, specific result showing successful property transfer via latent space arithmetic, which revises and broadens the original contribution into an explicit performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Generated sequences by interpolation between latent space data points demonstrate the ability of the AAE to generalize and produce meaningful biological sequences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Generated sequences by interpolation between latent space data points demonstrate the ability of the AAE to generalize and produce meaningful biological sequences.
alignments.rationale: The interpolation-generalization result is restated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study confirms the ability of deep learning frameworks to model biological complexity and provide new tools to explore amino acid sequence and functional spaces.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study confirms the ability of deep learning frameworks to model biological complexity and provide new tools for exploring amino acid sequence and functional spaces.
alignments.rationale: The concluding statement about DL frameworks and their utility is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning (DL) pipelines for 3D cell segmentation demonstrate different levels of accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis shows that deep learning pipelines have different levels of accuracy in segmenting 3D cellular images.
alignments.rationale: The publication directly reports the same finding that DL pipelines vary in accuracy for 3D cell segmentation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two end-to-end 3D DL methods designed for cell boundary detection show high performance and adaptability to new data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two end-to-end 3D deep learning methods designed for cell boundary detection demonstrate high performance and adaptability to new data.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same performance and adaptability result for the two end-to-end methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed benchmarking protocol allows for a quantitative comparison of DL segmentation pipelines using common datasets and evaluation metrics.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate a concrete benchmarking protocol; instead it emphasizes difficulties from diversity and lack of uniform evaluation, indicating the preprint's specific protocol claim was omitted or not retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DL pipelines may exhibit significant performance differences based on their model architecture and pipeline components.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The relative performances of different deep learning methods for 3D cell segmentation are difficult to define due to diversity and lack of uniform evaluation strategies.
alignments.rationale: The preprint framed observed performance differences as a conclusion; the publication reframes this as a challenge in defining relative performance, which narrows and tempers the original conclusion.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning pipelines provide high accuracy segmentation of cellular images and are considered state of the art for image segmentation problems.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a broader concluding statement that DL pipelines are state of the art and provide high accuracy, which was not explicitly asserted in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reading time on each word is predicted by the attention weights in transformer-based deep neural networks optimized for the reading task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The reading time on each word is predicted by the attention weights in transformer-based deep neural networks optimized for the reading task.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result verbatim; there is no change in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Eye-tracking reveals that readers separately attend to basic text features and question-relevant information during first-pass reading and rereading.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Eye tracking reveals that readers separately attend to basic text features and question-relevant information during first-pass reading and rereading.
alignments.rationale: The eye-tracking finding is reported unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Text features and question relevance separately modulate attention weights in shallow and deep DNN layers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Text features and question relevance separately modulate attention weights in shallow and deep DNN layers.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this layered modulation finding without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When readers scan a passage without a question in mind, their reading time is predicted by DNNs optimized for a word prediction task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: When readers scan a passage without a question in mind, their reading time is predicted by DNNs optimized for a word prediction task.
alignments.rationale: The prediction-by-word-prediction-models result is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study offers a computational account of how task optimization modulates attention distribution during real-world reading.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study offers a computational account of how task optimization modulates attention distribution during real-world reading.
alignments.rationale: The concluding computational account is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CANDO platform improves the efficacy and safety of drug discovery by computing interaction signatures between drugs and a large library of protein structures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CANDO platform improves the efficacy and safety of drug discovery by computing interaction signatures between drugs and a large library of protein structures.
alignments.rationale: The platform-level contribution is stated identically in both preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using a deep learning based autoencoder, the study generated novel drug-like compounds that are predicted to be significantly more behaviorally similar to approved and experimental drugs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: 16 out of 20 designed compounds were predicted to be significantly more behaviorally similar to approved and experimental drugs compared to controls.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a numeric quantification (16/20) of the preprint's behavioral similarity claim, clarifying but not contradicting the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Redesigns of objectives developed via rational drug design perform significantly better than those derived from natural sources.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Redesigns of objectives developed via rational drug design performed significantly better than those derived from natural sources.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the comparative performance claim about rational-design redesigns versus natural-source designs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Designed compounds are structurally diverse and synthetically feasible compared to their respective objective drugs while maintaining high predicted behavioral similarity.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate or provide evidence for the structural diversity and synthetic feasibility claim present in the preprint; this specific claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study generated new designs that enhance thirteen drugs/compounds associated with non-small cell lung cancer and anti-aging properties.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study generated new designs that enhanced thirteen drugs associated with non-small cell lung cancer and anti-aging properties.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same specific result about enhancing thirteen drugs linked to NSCLC and anti-aging.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Using a deep learning-based autoencoder, the study successfully reduced the dimensionality of drug-proteome interaction signatures.
alignments.rationale: The dimensionality-reduction phrasing is an explicit methodological/result claim present in the publication but not in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hippocampal replay trains generative models in the neocortex to recreate sensory experiences via latent variable representations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The computational model demonstrates how hippocampal replay trains generative models to recreate sensory experiences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core result that hippocampal replay trains generative models to recreate sensory experiences but omits anatomical ('neocortex') and mechanistic ('latent variable representations') details present in the preprint, thus a clarification and narrowing of scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model provides mechanisms for semantic memory, imagination, episodic future thinking, relational inference, and schema-based distortions including boundary extension. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Episodic memories are reconstructed and share neural substrates with imagination. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes reconstruction and overlap with imagination but does not enumerate the broader set of cognitive functions claimed in the preprint, indicating a narrowed/revised claim with a more tempered emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model explains how unique sensory and predictable conceptual elements of memories are stored and reconstructed by combining hippocampal and neocortical systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model explains the storage and reconstruction of unique sensory and predictable conceptual elements of memories. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the explanation of storage and reconstruction of sensory and conceptual memory elements but omits explicit reference to hippocampal–neocortical combination, narrowing mechanistic detail while keeping the explanatory claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simulations show effects of memory age and hippocampal lesions in agreement with previous models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simulations indicate effects of memory age and hippocampal lesions that align with previous models. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the simulation result about memory age and hippocampal lesion effects consistent with prior models with essentially the same content and interpretation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hippocampal replay training of neocortical generative models provides a comprehensive account of memory construction, imagination, and consolidation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The computational model demonstrates how hippocampal replay trains generative models to recreate sensory experiences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's broader concluding claim of a comprehensive account is softened in the publication to a more limited demonstration about replay training generative models; thus the claim is revised and narrowed with tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Elevated biological age and accelerated brain age are associated with an increased risk of multimorbidity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Elevated biological age and brain age are associated with an increased risk of multimorbidity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the association between elevated biological/brain age and multimorbidity with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study is the first to examine the relationship between accelerated brain age and multimorbidity. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study is the first to examine the relationship between accelerated brain age and multimorbidity. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the claim of novelty regarding accelerated brain age and multimorbidity unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research presents the first effort to test whether sex moderates the relationship between surrogate aging biomarkers and multimorbidity. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the claimed first test of sex moderation; instead it introduces a different 'first attempt' claim (mental health context), so the specific sex-moderation contribution is absent (removed) from the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings may contribute to new strategies to improve the treatment of multimorbidity and detection of at-risk individuals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings hold the potential to advance the understanding of disease accumulation and their relationship with aging. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the contribution as advancing understanding of disease accumulation rather than suggesting direct translational strategies for treatment/detection, so the claim is revised and somewhat tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A sufficiently simple CNN model trained with energetic features generalizes better than a pre-trained language model on out-of-distribution sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A sufficiently simple CNN model outperforms general pre-trained language models on out-of-distribution sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core empirical finding that a simple CNN outperforms pre-trained LMs on OOD sequences with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models trained on TS50 data could identify 18 residue positions and 5 identical amino-acid mutations in an independent mAb with available thermal melting temperatures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models trained on TS50 data could identify 18 residue positions and 5 identical amino-acid mutations in an independent mAb. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the specific result about identifying residue positions and identical mutations in an independent mAb unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These models can be broadly applicable for improving the biological characteristics of antibodies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models have potential applications in optimizing large-scale production and delivery of mAbs or bsAbs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the preprint's broad claim of improving biological characteristics to a more specific set of applications (production and delivery), so the claim is revised and its scope narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Transferring such models for alternative physico-chemical properties of scFvs can have potential applications in optimizing large-scale production and delivery of mAbs or bsAbs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models have potential applications in optimizing large-scale production and delivery of mAbs or bsAbs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the applied potential for production/delivery; it omits specific emphasis on transfer across physico-chemical properties, thereby clarifying and slightly narrowing the original wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning allows better dive prediction than usual methods such as Hidden Markov Models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning allows better dive prediction than usual methods such as Hidden Markov Models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the result verbatim: deep learning outperforms standard methods for dive prediction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trained networks demonstrate generalization properties for inferring dives distribution for seabirds from other colonies and ecosystems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The trained networks demonstrate generalization properties for inferring dives distribution for seabirds from other colonies and ecosystems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The generalization claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Convolutional networks trained on Peruvian boobies show great ability to predict dives of boobies from other colonies and distinct ecosystems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Convolutional networks trained on Peruvian boobies show great ability to predict dives of boobies from other colonies and distinct ecosystems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this specific result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using a transfer learning strategy known as ‘fine-tuning’ reduced the size of the dataset needed to accurately predict dives in a tropical booby from Brazil. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using a transfer learning strategy known as 'fine-tuning' reduced the size of the dataset needed to accurately predict dives in a tropical booby from Brazil. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The fine-tuning transfer learning result is directly carried over into the publication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The networks trained in this study will provide a relevant starting point for future fine-tuning works for seabird trajectory segmentation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The networks trained in this study will provide a relevant starting point for future fine-tuning works for seabird trajectory segmentation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim about providing a starting point for future work is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A fundamentally new framework improves computational efficiency by orders of magnitude for exploring parametric spaces in mechanistic models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework improves computational efficiency by orders of magnitude. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core contribution claim about large gains in computational efficiency unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trained neural network can explore system dynamics of a much larger parametric space using a limited number of simulations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A neural network can be trained using a limited number of simulations generated by a mechanistic model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication states the same capability but with slightly different phrasing emphasizing training with limited simulations; content and scope remain effectively unchanged, considered a clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework can predict 1-D distributions and probability density functions of variables of interest with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The trained neural network can explore a much larger parametric space. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim that the trained network can explore larger parametric spaces; both statements reflect the model's predictive accuracy and exploration capability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using an ensemble of neural networks allows for the self-contained evaluation of the quality of each prediction. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using an ensemble of neural networks allows for the self-contained evaluation of the quality of each prediction. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the ensemble-based evaluation claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The work can potentially serve as a platform for faster parametric space screening of biological models with user-defined objectives. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work provides a platform for fast parametric space screening of biological models with user defined objectives. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the concluding claim that the framework serves as a platform for rapid parametric screening with user-defined objectives.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An automated deep learning approach was developed to segment the heart and differentiate normal from malformed hearts in mouse μCT scans.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pipeline will accelerate diagnosis of heart anomalies in mice and facilitate mechanistic studies of congenital heart defects.
alignments.rationale: The preprint claim of developing an automated approach is reframed in the publication as an impact statement about accelerating diagnosis and enabling studies; the core contribution is preserved but presented with broader impact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The self-configurating neural network achieved a Dice coefficient of 96% when validated against expert segmentations.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the Dice segmentation metric in its summarized claims, so the specific segmentation-performance claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The diagnosis model demonstrated a sensitivity of 92%, specificity of 96%, and area under the curve (AUC) of 97% in the initial cohort.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning approach achieves an area-under-the-curve (AUC) of 97% on a cohort of 139 μCT scans of control and mutant mice.
alignments.rationale: Both statements report the same AUC (97%) on the initial cohort, but the publication omits the sensitivity/specificity details and adds cohort size, so the claim is preserved with clarified reporting.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Performance on the prospective cohort was excellent, with a sensitivity of 92%, specificity of 100%, and AUC of 100%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Performance on a similar ‘prospective’ cohort is excellent with an AUC of 100%.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the key result (AUC 100%) for the prospective cohort but omits per-class sensitivity/specificity values, representing a clarification of reported metrics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A user-friendly Napari plugin was created to facilitate the adoption and retraining of the segmentation and diagnosis models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The user-friendly Napari plugin allows researchers without coding expertise to utilize and retrain the model.
alignments.rationale: Both claims describe the same Napari plugin and its purpose (use and retraining by non-coders); wording and emphasis are effectively unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A biosensor-based enzyme engineering method can be applied to machine learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state this generalizable conclusion as an independent claim; related points are present but this specific framing is not explicit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two cycles of machine learning-assisted directed evolution resulted in the acquisition of XylM-D140E-V144K-F243L-N244S with 15 times higher productivity than wild-type XylM.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two cycles of machine learning-assisted directed evolution resulted in the acquisition of XylM-D140E-V144K-F243L-N244S with 15 times higher productivity than wild-type XylM.
alignments.rationale: The result is reported identically in the publication, preserving the numeric improvement and variant identity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An indirect enzyme activity evaluation method using biosensors is sufficiently quantitative and high-throughput to be used as training data for machine learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An indirect enzyme activity evaluation method using biosensors is sufficiently quantitative and high-throughput to be used as training data for machine learning.
alignments.rationale: This methodological claim is carried forward verbatim into the publication, retaining its role as a training-data source for ML.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings expand the versatility of machine learning in enzyme engineering.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings expand the versatility of machine learning in enzyme engineering.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates this broader conclusion about ML versatility in enzyme engineering without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Biologically informed neural networks (BINNs), also known as visible neural networks (VNNs), have emerged as a solution for biomarker discovery and drug response prediction in personalized medicine.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BINNs/VNNs represent a promising approach for integrating biological knowledge into predictive models for personalized medicine.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes a similar conclusion about BINNs/VNNs as a promising approach for personalized medicine, reframing the specific 'solution for biomarker discovery and drug response prediction' into a more general promise to integrate biological knowledge.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BINNs enhance interpretability by embedding prior knowledge from gene ontologies pathway databases into their architecture.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BINNs/VNNs represent a promising approach for integrating biological knowledge into predictive models for personalized medicine.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes integrating biological knowledge (which underlies interpretability), but does not explicitly foreground interpretability via ontology-embedded architectures, so the preprint claim is preserved in a more general form.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The systematic review identifies 86 recent papers implementing BINNs and highlights key trends in architectural design decisions, data sources, and methods for evaluation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis reveals a growing adoption of biologically informed neural networks (BINNs) or visible neural networks (VNNs).
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the review's high-level finding of growing adoption but omits the specific paper count and detailed trend items in the summarized claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The growth in popularity of BINNs is mitigated by a lack of standardized terminology, tools, and benchmarks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a lack of standardized terminology, computational tools, and benchmarks in the implementation of BINNs/VNNs.
alignments.rationale: Both statements identify the same deficiencies (terminology, tools, benchmarks) that hinder the field; wording is essentially identical in substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HYFA achieves superior performance over existing transcriptome imputation methods, especially when multiple reference tissues are available.
alignments.pub_claim_text: HYFA achieves superior performance over existing methods, especially when multiple reference tissues are available.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the superior performance claim with the same conditional emphasis on availability of multiple reference tissues, preserving the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HYFA can accurately resolve cell-type signatures from bulk gene expression, even in tissues that were never observed in the training set.
alignments.pub_claim_text: HYFA is a parameter-efficient graph representation learning approach for joint imputation of multi-tissue and cell-type gene expression.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames HYFA as a method for joint multi-tissue and cell-type imputation, which supports the preprint's claim about resolving cell-type signatures; the publication emphasizes the method class and efficiency rather than the exact extrapolation claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The metagenes learned by HYFA capture information about known biological pathways.
alignments.rationale: The publication's summarized claims do not mention metagenes capturing pathway information, so this more specific interpretability claim is not present in the listed publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The HYFA-imputed dataset can be used to identify regulatory genetic variations (eQTLs), with substantial gains over the original incomplete dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The HYFA-imputed dataset can be used to identify replicable regulatory genetic variations, with substantial gains over the original incomplete dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim that HYFA-imputed data aids identification of regulatory variants (eQTLs), emphasizing replicability but keeping the same substantive result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HYFA is genotype-agnostic and supports a variable number of collected tissues per individual.
alignments.pub_claim_text: HYFA is genotype agnostic and supports a variable number of collected tissues per individual.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this capability claim directly, indicating no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reinforcement learning performance in older individuals remains intact even under higher task demands.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Older adults performed less efficiently initially on a complex navigation task compared to young adults.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's broad claim that performance remains intact is revised in the publication to note initial inefficiency in older adults on a complex task, representing a tempering and narrowing of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most behavioral parameters and learning and exploration rates did not show group differences between older and younger participants.
alignments.pub_claim_text: With sufficient trials, older adults performed as well as young adults on the navigation task.
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies that performance parity is reached given enough trials, which aligns with the preprint's observation of no group differences in many behavioral parameters across learning/exploration when measured over sufficient exposures.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Task demand can explain mixed results in reinforcement learning performance across different age groups.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ageing does not universally impair performance on higher-level cognitive tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication's generalized conclusion that ageing does not universally impair higher-level tasks is consistent with the preprint's explanation that task demand moderates observed age-related differences, though the publication frames it as a broader takeaway.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Publishing null results is important to prevent the stigmatization of older individuals regarding performance deficits.
alignments.rationale: The publication's summarized claims do not include this normative statement about publishing null results and stigma, so the claim is not present among the listed publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Allelic-effect annotations constructed using deep learning models are highly enriched for disease heritability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The annotations from deep learning models were highly enriched for disease heritability.
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication language is effectively identical about allelic-effect annotations being highly enriched for disease heritability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Only limited conditionally significant results were produced by the allelic-effect annotations in the context of complex diseases.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report this claim about only limited conditionally significant results; the specific negative result is absent from the published claim set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning models have not yet achieved their full potential to provide a considerable amount of unique information for complex diseases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning models have yet to achieve their full potential to provide considerable unique information for complex disease.
alignments.rationale: Wording and meaning are effectively the same that models have yet to reach full potential for unique information in complex disease.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The informativeness of deep learning models for disease cannot be inferred from metrics based on their accuracy in predicting regulatory annotations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The conditional informativeness for disease cannot be inferred from the accuracy in predicting regulatory annotations.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert the same conclusion that predictive-accuracy metrics do not imply conditional informativeness for disease.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning models have shown promise in predicting regulatory effects from DNA sequence.
alignments.rationale: This general claim about promise in predicting regulatory effects appears in the publication but was not stated among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nonlinear reweighting of energy terms from Rosetta, through the use of machine learning, exhibits improved predictability of ΔΔG values associated with interfacial mutations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nonlinear reweighting of energy terms from Rosetta improves predictability of ΔΔG values associated with interfacial mutations.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about improved ΔΔG predictability via nonlinear reweighting.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Accurate predictions of the effects of interfacial mutations to identify 'hotspots' have remained elusive despite various modeling and machine learning methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Accurate predictions of the effects of interfacial mutations to identify 'hotspots' have remained elusive despite various modeling and machine learning methods.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about the elusiveness of accurate hotspot prediction is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Protein-protein interfaces play essential roles in a variety of biological processes and many therapeutic molecules are targeted at these interfaces.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Therapeutic molecules are often targeted at protein-protein interfaces due to their essential roles in biological processes.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the same contribution linking essential roles of interfaces to their being therapeutic targets; meaning is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Janggu is a python library that facilitates deep learning for genomics applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Janggu is a python library that facilitates deep learning for genomics applications.
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Janggu improves the performance of models by allowing the inclusion of high-order sequence features.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Janggu improves the performance of models by allowing the inclusion of high-order sequence features.
alignments.rationale: The performance-improvement claim via high-order features appears unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Janggu enables computational biologists to assess biological hypotheses more rapidly.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Janggu is expected to significantly reduce repetitive programming overhead for deep learning applications in genomics.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the 'faster hypothesis assessment' claim as reduction of programming overhead — a clarification of the mechanism enabling faster work rather than a direct claim about hypothesis testing speed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Janggu provides a unified and flexible data acquisition and pre-processing framework for genomics data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework of Janggu allows for the evaluation of different model topologies for predicting binding sites for the transcription factor JunD.
alignments.rationale: The general claim about a unified flexible framework is instantiated in the publication by a specific evaluation capability (JunD binding models), which narrows the generality while clarifying an applied use case.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Janggu enables the visualization of predictions as genomic tracks or by exporting them to the bigWig format.
alignments.rationale: Visualization/export capabilities are described in the publication but were not listed among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GrID-Net increases variant coverage by 36% when applied to schizophrenia genetic variants.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GrID-Net increases variant coverage by 36% when applied to schizophrenia genetic variants.
alignments.rationale: The numerical result and context are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GrID-Net uncovers noncoding mechanisms that dysregulate 132 genes, including key potassium transporters such as KCNG2 and SLC12A6.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GrID-Net uncovers noncoding mechanisms that dysregulate 132 genes, including key potassium transporters such as KCNG2 and SLC12A6.
alignments.rationale: The result describing dysregulation of 132 genes and examples is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides evidence for the prominent role of neural transcription-factor binding disruptions in schizophrenia etiology.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides evidence for the prominent role of neural transcription-factor binding disruptions in schizophrenia etiology.
alignments.rationale: The study conclusion about TF-binding disruptions and schizophrenia is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The concept of 'cell-state parallax' allows for the inference of causal mechanisms from static single-cell snapshots.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The concept of 'cell-state parallax' can be used to infer causal mechanisms from static single-cell snapshots.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conceptual claim with minor wording change but equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The work underscores the breakthrough potential of cell-state parallax in single-cell multiomics for discovering tissue-specific gene regulatory mechanisms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work underscores the breakthrough potential of cell-state parallax in single-cell multiomics for discovering tissue-specific gene regulatory mechanisms.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim about breakthrough potential is preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Graph Partitioned GNN (GP-GNN) architecture effectively partitions graphs during the prediction process to focus on subgraphs containing the most relevant information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GP-GNN framework effectively partitions GNN graphs to focus on the most relevant subgraphs.
alignments.rationale: The contribution about effective partitioning to focus on relevant subgraphs is retained with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GP-GNN can accurately predict Liquid-Liquid Phase Separation (LLPS) by partitioning protein graphs into task-relevant subgraphs, such as those highlighting intrinsically disordered regions (IDRs).
alignments.pub_claim_text: GP-GNN accurately predicts protein liquid-liquid phase separation (LLPS) by partitioning protein graphs into task-relevant subgraphs consistent with known intrinsically disordered regions (IDRs).
alignments.rationale: The result about accurate LLPS prediction via task-relevant subgraph partitioning, and consistency with IDRs, is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The GP-GNN model achieves state-of-the-art accuracy in predicting LLPS for both regulator and scaffold proteins.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieves state-of-the-art accuracy in predicting LLPS.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports state-of-the-art LLPS accuracy but omits the explicit regulator/scaffold protein qualifier, narrowing the scope slightly while keeping the core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Subgraphs identified by the GP-GNN are consistent with annotated intrinsically disordered regions (IDRs).
alignments.pub_claim_text: GP-GNN accurately predicts protein liquid-liquid phase separation (LLPS) by partitioning protein graphs into task-relevant subgraphs consistent with known intrinsically disordered regions (IDRs).
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states consistency of task-relevant subgraphs with known IDRs, matching this preprint observation.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The insights provided by the model are valuable for downstream biological investigations.
alignments.rationale: This broader claim about downstream value appears in the publication but was not present among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dopamine activity encodes sensory prediction errors unrelated to reward. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Midbrain dopamine neurons encode sensory prediction errors unrelated to reward. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The wording and meaning are essentially identical; both state that dopamine encodes sensory prediction errors independent of reward.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dopamine supports a form of reinforcement learning that lies between model-based and model-free algorithms. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dopamine supports a form of reinforcement learning that lies between model-based and model-free algorithms. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dopamine transients correspond with reward prediction errors while also signaling errors in sensory predictions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dopamine transients correspond with reward prediction errors while also playing a role in sensory preconditioning and identity unblocking. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the link to reward prediction errors but replaces the general phrase 'signaling errors in sensory predictions' with specific phenomena (sensory preconditioning and identity unblocking), making the claim more specific.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new theory of dopamine function accounts for phenomena such as sensory preconditioning and identity unblocking. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dopamine transients correspond with reward prediction errors while also playing a role in sensory preconditioning and identity unblocking. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both statements connect dopamine function to sensory preconditioning and identity unblocking; the publication frames this in terms of observed dopamine transient roles, clarifying the link between the theory and those phenomena.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TarDis outperforms existing methods in data integration, covariate disentanglement, and robust out-of-distribution predictions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TarDis outperforms existing methods in data integration, covariate disentanglement, and robust out-of-distribution predictions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model's capacity to produce interpretable and structured latent spaces enhances its utility in hypothesis-driven research. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model's capacity to produce interpretable and structured latent spaces enhances its utility in hypothesis-driven research. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The content is the same, but the publication reduces stated certainty (high -> medium), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Disentangled latent spaces allow researchers to computationally predict cellular responses at drug dosages or developmental stages that were never experimentally observed. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly claim the ability to predict unobserved dosages/stages; while related capabilities are implied, the specific predictive claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Preserving the continuous nature of covariates in disentangled representations is critical for maintaining their intrinsic ordering and discerning nuanced biological shifts. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TarDis effectively generates multiple latent-space representations that capture each continuous and categorical target covariate separately, along with unexplained variation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the capability to capture continuous covariates (matching the preprint's emphasis) but frames it as an achieved result rather than stressing the normative claim that preserving continuity is 'critical', thereby clarifying the emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tailored deep generative model systematically separates both categorical and continuous covariates into independent latent dimensions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TarDis effectively generates multiple latent-space representations that capture each continuous and categorical target covariate separately, along with unexplained variation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the model's ability to separate categorical and continuous covariates into distinct latent representations, matching the preprint contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Responses to natural stimuli in area V4 are well predicted by features from convolutional neural networks trained on image classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Responses to natural stimuli in area V4 are well predicted by features from convolutional neural networks trained on image classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claims in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: V4 was best predicted by semantic classification features and exhibited high task selectivity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: V4 was best predicted by semantic classification features and exhibited high task selectivity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct match in wording and substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Diverse non-semantic tasks explained aspects of V4 function beyond those captured by individual semantic tasks. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Diverse non-semantic tasks explained aspects of V4 function that are not captured by individual semantic tasks. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same content with minor wording change; meaning unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Jointly considering the features of a pair of semantic classification tasks was sufficient to yield one of the top V4 models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Jointly considering the features of a pair of semantic classification tasks was sufficient to yield one of the top V4 models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: V4’s affinity to 2D or 3D stimulus properties can result from semantic functional goals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: V4's selectivity to 2D or 3D stimulus properties can result from semantic functional goals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same conceptual conclusion expressed in slightly different wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ABTrans is a Transformer-based model that was developed for the first time to predict the interaction between anti-Aβ antibodies and peptides. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the model and its results but does not assert the novelty claim that it was the first Transformer developed for this task; that specific novelty statement is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ABTrans achieved an accuracy of 0.8278 and is capable of determining the four levels of binding ability between antibody and Aβ: not binding, weak binding, medium binding, and strong binding. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning model ABTrans achieved an accuracy of 0.83 in classifying the binding ability between anti-Aβ antibodies and dodecapeptides. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports essentially the same accuracy and classification capability (rounded value), matching the preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Aducanumab and Donanemab have the least cross-reactions with other human amyloidogenic proteins. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Aducanumab and Donanemab exhibited the least cross-reactivity with other human amyloidogenic proteins. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is the same but the publication reports it with higher certainty, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ABTrans has potential applications in predicting off-target and cross-reactivity effects of antibodies and in designing new anti-Aβ antibodies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study systematically screened interactions between eleven selected anti-Aβ antibodies and all human proteins to identify potential off-target candidates. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the systematic screening performed (concrete contribution) which clarifies and grounds the preprint's proposed potential applications in specific study actions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A high fraction of generated peptides are functional and possess features important for mitochondrial targeting. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A high fraction of the generated peptides (90.14%) are functional and possess features important for mitochondrial targeting. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication quantifies the same result but otherwise restates the same claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Artificial mitochondrial targeting sequences (MTSs) displayed significant diversity, sharing less than 60% sequence identity with MTSs in the UniProt database. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include an explicit claim about sequence-identity diversity to UniProt MTSs; that specific result is not reported.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of artificial MTSs increased titers of 3-hydroxypropionic acid through pathway compartmentalization and improved 5-aminolevulinate synthase delivery by 1.62-fold and 4.76-fold, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The artificial peptides improve 5-aminolevulinate synthase delivery by 1.62-fold and increase 3-hydroxypropionic acid titers through pathway compartmentalization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same quantitative improvements and effects.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work demonstrates the potential of generative artificial intelligence in designing novel, functional mitochondrial targeting sequences. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our work demonstrates the potential of generative artificial intelligence for both fundamental research and practical applications in mitochondrial biology. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the claim about AI potential but broadens it to explicitly include both fundamental research and practical applications, clarifying and extending the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Whole-brain data analysis revealed a classification accuracy of 82.03% distinguishing groups within ∼50 ms after speech onset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: For clear speech perception, whole-brain data revealed a classification accuracy of 81.50%, distinguishing groups within approximately 60 ms after speech onset.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same core result but with slightly different numeric values and a slightly later latency (60 ms vs ~50 ms), so the claim is clarified rather than changed in substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lower accuracy of 78.39% and delayed classification performance were observed when speech tokens were embedded in noise.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lower accuracy of 78.12% and delayed classification performance were observed when speech was embedded in noise, with group segregation at 80 ms.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same finding with slightly adjusted accuracy and a specific delayed latency (80 ms), so this is a clarification of the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Left hemisphere speech activity was better at distinguishing hearing groups than activity measured over the right hemisphere.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Left hemisphere speech activity was better at distinguishing hearing groups than activity measured in the right hemisphere.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is effectively identical between preprint and publication with no substantive change in wording or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Stability selection analysis identified 13 brain regions where source activity segregated groups with >80% accuracy for clear speech.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Stability selection analysis identified 12 brain regions where source activity segregated groups with over 80% accuracy for clear speech.
alignments.rationale: The core claim remains but the number of regions was reduced from 13 to 12 in the publication, a revision that narrows the anatomical scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A larger number of neural regions, particularly in the right hemisphere and frontal lobe, are active when processing degraded speech information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A larger number of active areas were confirmed in the right hemisphere when processing noise-degraded speech information.
alignments.rationale: Both versions report more right-hemisphere activity for degraded speech, but the publication omits the frontal-lobe emphasis present in the preprint, narrowing the anatomical claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The end-to-end workflow for conducting near real-time monitoring of wading birds in the Everglades enables imagery acquisition as frequently as weekly.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The end-to-end workflow for conducting near real-time monitoring of wading birds in the Everglades enables imagery acquisition as frequently as weekly.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computer vision model provides accurate object detection, species classification, and both total and species-level counts for five out of six target species.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The computer vision model used in the workflow provides accurate object detection and species classification for five out of six target species.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same model performance for five of six species; core result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Data on the populations of these species is available in near real-time, specifically in less than one week from the date of the survey.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Data on the populations of the monitored species is available in near real-time, specifically in less than one week from the date of the survey.
alignments.rationale: The timing and availability claim is reiterated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model performed poorly for Snowy Egrets due to the small number of labels and difficulty distinguishing them from White Ibis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model performed poorly for Snowy Egrets due to the small number of labels and difficulty distinguishing them from White Ibis.
alignments.rationale: The limitation is stated the same way in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Perceiver architecture demonstrates effectiveness in predicting mRNA and protein levels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Perceiver-type models demonstrate effectiveness in predicting both mRNA and protein levels across various biological samples.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the general effectiveness but emphasizes applicability across various biological samples, broadening the original general claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed DNAPerceiver model predicts mRNA levels from the sequence straddling the Transcription Start Site (TSS).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Perceiver model effectively predicts mRNA levels from the sequence upstream and downstream of the Transcription Start Site (TSS).
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same technical contribution (predicting mRNA from sequences around the TSS) with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ProteinPerceiver model predicts protein levels from the protein sequence.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ProteinPerceiver model successfully predicts protein levels from the protein sequence.
alignments.rationale: The contribution and result for ProteinPerceiver are restated equivalently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Protein&DNAPerceiver model predicts protein levels from TSS-straddling and protein sequences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Protein&DNAPerceiver model can predict protein levels from TSS and protein sequences.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same combined-model capability with essentially identical wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model achieves state-of-the-art performance for mRNA level prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Perceiver-type models demonstrate effectiveness in predicting both mRNA and protein levels across various biological samples.
alignments.rationale: The publication omits an explicit state-of-the-art claim and instead reports general effectiveness, indicating the performance superiority claim was toned down (revised and tempered).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MarkerGeneBERT successfully extracted 8873 cell markers of 1733 cell types in 435 human tissues/subtissues and 9064 cell markers of 1832 cell types in 492 mouse tissues/subtissues.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report these specific extraction counts and so the exact quantitative extraction claim from the preprint was not carried forward.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method achieved 76% completeness and 75% accuracy when compared with existing manually curated cell marker databases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MarkerGeneBERT achieved 76% completeness and 75% accuracy in extracting cell markers from single-cell sequencing studies.
alignments.rationale: The performance metrics are reported identically in the publication, matching the preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identified 89 cell types and 183 marker genes that were not available in the existing cell marker database.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study unveiled 89 cell types and 183 marker genes that were absent from existing databases.
alignments.rationale: The discovery of previously absent cell types and marker genes is retained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The annotation of brain tissue single-cell sequencing data using the compiled list of brain tissue marker genes was consistent with the results of original studies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The compiled brain tissue marker gene list from MarkerGeneBERT yielded results consistent with original studies when applied to annotate scRNA-seq data.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates that annotation using the compiled list matches original study results, consistent with the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study illustrates how systematic application of NLP-based methods could expedite and enhance the annotation and interpretation of scRNA-seq data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings underscore the efficacy of NLP-based methods in expediting and augmenting the annotation and interpretation of scRNA-seq data.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the concluding claim about the value of NLP for scRNA-seq annotation and interpretation with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NLP-based approaches have superior accuracy in identifying NAFLD within the EHR compared to ICD/text search-based approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: NLP outperformed ICD and text search in both sensitivity and F2 score for identifying patients with NAFLD.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the performance metrics (sensitivity and F2) supporting the preprint's general superiority claim, clarifying but not changing its substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Suspected NAFLD on imaging is often not acknowledged in subsequent clinical documentation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Analysis of information flows demonstrated loss of key information that could have helped prevent the progression of early NAFLD to NASH or cirrhosis.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the preprint observation about lack of acknowledgement as part of broader information-flow losses; this clarifies the systems-level consequence of unacknowledged imaging findings.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Many patients with suspected NAFLD documented in radiology notes are later found to have more advanced liver disease.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many patients with NAFLD documented only in radiology notes were later found to have more advanced liver disease.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this finding directly and quantitatively aligns with the preprint's observation about progression.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For identification of NAFLD, NLP performed better than alternative selection modalities and facilitated follow-on analysis of information flow.
alignments.pub_claim_text: NLP-based approaches were more accurate at identifying NAFLD within the EHR than ICD/text search-based approaches.
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on the accuracy advantage of NLP (echoing the preprint) but does not explicitly restate the facilitation of follow-on information-flow analyses, so the claim is clarified toward the accuracy result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach allows for estimating model parameters and assessing their identifiability with only partial knowledge of the system structure. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach allows for the estimation of model parameters and assessment of their identifiability with only partial knowledge of the system structure. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Wording and meaning are effectively identical; both state the same capability (parameter estimation and identifiability with partial system knowledge).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrating neural networks into the model structure introduces challenges for parameter estimation, including the need for global exploration of the search space and assessment of parameter identifiability. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrating neural networks into the model addresses the challenges of global exploration of the mechanistic parameter space and potential loss of parameter identifiability. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both claims concern NN integration and global exploration/identifiability, but the preprint frames these as challenges introduced while the paper claims those challenges are addressed — a substantive change in claim polarity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of the proposed workflow was assessed on test cases derived from three different benchmark models designed to mimic real-world conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pipeline performance is evaluated on three test cases that replicate real-world conditions, including noisy data and limited system observability. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both state evaluation on three test cases; the publication adds specifics (noisy data, limited observability), which clarifies but does not change the core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The workflow represents a first step in adapting traditional methods used in fully mechanistic models to hybrid modeling scenarios. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not present an explicit claim framing the workflow as a 'first step' toward adapting traditional methods to hybrid models, so this preprint conclusion appears omitted in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A neural network trained on immunoglobulin G binding to a large set of amino acid sequences can effectively aggregate sequence-binding information and improve the accuracy of disease classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using a neural network to model the sequence dependence of molecular recognition improves the accuracy of array-based classification of disease compared to raw binding data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims state that a NN modelling sequence dependence improves classification accuracy relative to raw binding; the publication frames it more explicitly but the core result is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning-based approach enhances the diagnostic power of peptide array-based antibody profiles by incorporating sequence information from each peptide. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using a neural network to model the sequence dependence of molecular recognition improves the accuracy of array-based classification of disease compared to raw binding data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's general conclusion about enhanced diagnostic power is operationalized in the publication as an explicit accuracy improvement versus raw data, clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of high density arrays of near-random peptide sequences allows for a general, disease agnostic approach to diagnosis by analyzing antibody binding patterns in serum. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The immunosignature approach allows detection and discrimination of humoral immune responses to various infectious diseases using high density peptide arrays. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasizes a disease-agnostic, general diagnostic approach, whereas the publication frames results specifically for detection/discrimination across infectious diseases, narrowing and making the claim more specific and somewhat more cautious.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network model condenses disease differentiating information into a compact form suitable for classification or unsupervised clustering applications. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The output layer of the neural network model provides a highly condensed representation of the differential information between samples for classification or unsupervised clustering applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both describe the NN output as a highly condensed representation useful for classification or clustering; wording differs slightly but meaning is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A semi-supervised artificial neural network identified significantly more true human in vivo associations than interpreting mouse experiments directly. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Semi-supervised training of a neural network identified significantly more true human biological associations than interpreting mouse experiments directly. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claims are effectively identical in result and emphasis on semi-supervised NN outperforming direct interpretation of mouse experiments.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When prospectively evaluating biological associations in mouse studies, semi-supervised learning approaches provide the most accurate assessment of human in vivo disease and therapeutic mechanisms. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: When prospectively evaluating biological associations in mouse studies, semi-supervised learning approaches provide the most accurate assessment of human in vivo disease processes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both state the same conclusion about semi-supervised approaches offering the most accurate assessments when evaluating mouse-to-human associations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The task of translating insights from model systems to human disease contexts may be better accomplished by the use of systems modeling driven approaches. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The task of translating insights from model systems to human disease contexts may be better accomplished by a combination of translation-minded experimental design and computational approaches. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the recommended solution from solely systems-modeling approaches to a combined strategy including experimental design and computational methods, broadening the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Computational generalization of signaling insights from mouse to human contexts substantially improves upon direct generalization of mouse experimental insights. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Semi-supervised training of a neural network identified significantly more true human biological associations than interpreting mouse experiments directly. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This preprint result (computational generalization improves over direct generalization) aligns closely with the publication result about semi-supervised NN identifying more true human associations, so the substantive finding is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Participants who experienced greater task-induced anxiety avoided choices associated with punishment, even when this resulted in lower overall reward. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Participants who experienced greater task-induced anxiety avoided choices associated with punishment, even when this resulted in lower overall reward. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical wording and meaning between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Computational modelling revealed that the effect of avoidance was explained by greater individual sensitivities to punishment relative to rewards. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Computational modelling revealed that the effect of avoidance was explained by greater individual sensitivities to punishment relative to rewards. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct match in content and framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings were replicated in an independent sample and showed fair-to-excellent reliability of measures of task performance in a sub-sample retested one week later. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings were replicated in an independent sample and showed fair-to-excellent reliability of measures of task performance in a sub-sample retested 1 week later. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same finding with only minor formatting difference (one week vs 1 week).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates the potential of approach-avoidance reinforcement learning tasks as translational and computational models of anxiety-related avoidance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates the potential of approach-avoidance reinforcement learning tasks as translational and computational models of anxiety-related avoidance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Male grackles finish 'relearning' faster than female grackles when reward contingencies reverse. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Male grackles finish 'relearning' faster than female grackles when reward contingencies reverse. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result is verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Male grackles exhibit more pronounced risk-sensitive learning compared to females, as indicated by their choice behaviour being governed more strongly by recent foraging payoffs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Male grackles' choice behaviour is governed more strongly by the 'weight' of relative differences in recent foraging payoffs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same result expressed with slightly different phrasing about the behavioral weight on payoff differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Agent-based forward simulations of reinforcement learning replicate the observed sex-difference behavioural data in grackles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Agent-based forward simulations of reinforcement learning replicate the observed sex-difference behavioural data in grackles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct match between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Natural selection should favour risk-sensitive learning in urban-like environments, which are stable but stochastic. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Natural selection should favour risk-sensitive learning in hypothesised urban-like environments. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication retains the core evolutionary conclusion but omits the additional descriptor 'stable but stochastic', thus slightly clarifying/streamlining the environmental characterization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Risk-sensitive learning is a winning strategy for urban-invasion leaders, highlighting the role of life history and cognition in invasion success. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Risk-sensitive learning is a winning strategy for urban-invasion leaders, highlighting the role of life history and cognition in invasion success. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim preserved exactly between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Losartan significantly reduced learning rates from aversive events when participants were first exposed to the novel task environment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Losartan significantly reduced learning rates from aversive events when participants were first exposed to the novel task environment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication text are effectively identical in wording and certainty about this specific result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Losartan preserves learning from positive outcomes while reducing learning from negative events. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Losartan preserved learning from positive outcomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication splits the composite preprint claim into separate claims (positive preservation appears in Q1 while reduction of negative learning appears in Q0), so the publication representation clarifies the components rather than contradicting them.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Losartan enhances learning from positive relative to negative events. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Losartan enhances learning from positive relative to negative events. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The core comparative effect is the same between preprint and publication; wording and conclusion remain consistent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effects of losartan may represent a neurocognitive mechanism that enhances the effect of exposure therapy in clinical populations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effect of losartan may represent a computationally defined neurocognitive mechanism that could enhance the effect of exposure in clinical populations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication adds the phrase 'computationally defined' but conveys the same tentative clinical implication, clarifying the conceptual framing without changing the conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep-learning method achieved an average accuracy of 93% across bacterial species and antibiotics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep-learning method achieved an average accuracy of 93% across bacterial species and antibiotics after training on three million AST results. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds experimental detail (training set size) but preserves the same reported accuracy, so the claim is a clarified version of the preprint result.
