alignments.rationale: The publication restates the zero-shot function prediction claim and its competitive performance, matching the preprint assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Evo excels at multi-element generation tasks, generating synthetic CRISPR-Cas molecular complexes and entire transposable systems for the first time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Evo represents the first examples of protein-RNA and protein-DNA codesign with a language model.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the generation claim as examples of protein–nucleic-acid codesign, which changes the emphasis and narrows the original broader claim about specific molecular systems.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Evo can predict gene essentiality at nucleotide resolution and generate coding-rich sequences up to 650 kb in length, orders of magnitude longer than previous methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Evo learns how small mutations affect whole-organism fitness.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the model's ability to learn mutation effects on organismal fitness, which aligns with and clarifies the preprint's nucleotide-resolution essentiality prediction aspect; the long-sequence generation aspect is represented elsewhere or less emphasized in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neurons in the cerebellar nuclei and substantia nigra pars reticulata exhibited distinct temporal response patterns, with a fast excitatory response in the cerebellum synchronized with an inhibitory response in the basal ganglia.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neurons in the cerebellar nuclei and substantia nigra pars reticulata exhibited distinct temporal response patterns.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the observed distinct temporal response patterns; the more detailed timing in the preprint is consistent with the publication's result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Successful learning in the developed reservoir-based reinforcement learning model depended on synaptic plasticity in both the cerebellum and basal ganglia with a temporal precision on the order of 10 ms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Successful learning in the reservoir-based reinforcement learning model depended on synaptic plasticity in both the cerebellum and basal ganglia with a temporal precision on the order of 10 ms.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the model-dependence and temporal-precision claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cortical β-oscillations enhanced learning, and optimal reinforcement learning occurred when the output of cerebellar and basal ganglia signals were phase-locked at the frequency of cortical oscillation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cortical β-oscillations enhanced learning and optimal reinforcement learning occurred when the output of cerebellar and basal ganglia signals were phase-locked at the frequency of cortical oscillation.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the role of cortical β-oscillations and phase-locking in enhancing learning, matching the preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The coordinated output of the cerebellum and basal ganglia, driven by tightly tuned cortical input, underlies brain-wide synergistic reinforcement learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The coordinated output of the cerebellum and basal ganglia, driven by tightly tuned cortical input, underlies brain-wide synergistic reinforcement learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the high-level conclusion about coordinated outputs and synergistic reinforcement learning unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed FCNsignal framework can predict TF-DNA binding signals at the base-resolution level.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed FCNsignal framework can predict TF-DNA binding signals at the base-resolution level.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core contribution exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FCNsignal outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods on 53 TF ChIP-seq datasets and 6 chromatin accessibility ATAC-seq datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FCNsignal outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods based on experimental results from 53 TF ChIP-seq datasets and 6 chromatin accessibility ATAC-seq datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the performance claim using the same dataset counts and experimental framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework can locate all potential TF-DNA binding regions from DNA sequences of arbitrary length.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework can locate all potential TF-DNA binding regions on a whole chromosome and predict DNA sequences of arbitrary length.
alignments.rationale: The publication extends the claim to whole-chromosome localization and retains prediction on arbitrary-length sequences, broadening and clarifying the preprint's scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FCNsignal demonstrates the potential ability to discover causal disease-associated single-nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs).
alignments.pub_claim_text: FCNsignal demonstrates the potential ability to discover causal disease-associated single-nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs).
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the potential for SNP discovery without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The cascaded-CNN model predicts secondary structure elements, backbone structure, and Cα atoms, producing a complete prediction map.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The cascaded-CNN model predicts secondary structure elements, backbone structure, and Cα atoms, producing a complete prediction map.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the methodological claim directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This method accurately predicted 88.5% of the Cα atoms within 3Å of a protein’s backbone structure, surpassing the 66.8% mark achieved by the leading alternate method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This method accurately predicted 88.9% of the Cα atoms within 3 Å of a protein’s backbone structure, surpassing the 66.8% mark achieved by the leading alternate method.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a slightly updated numeric result (88.9% vs 88.5%), a minor clarification of the original performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The C-CNN achieved an average RMSD of 1.23Å for all 50 experimental density maps, which is similar to the Phenix based fully automatic method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The C-CNN achieved an average root-mean-square deviation (RMSD) of 1.24 Å on a set of 50 experimental density maps.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a very similar numeric RMSD (1.24 vs 1.23 Å) and reformulates the wording; this is a small clarification of the reported metric.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The cascaded-CNN outperformed several state-of-the-art prediction methods by producing the most complete prediction models, as measured by the percentage of found Cα atoms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method was tested on 50 experimental maps between 2.6 Å and 4.4 Å resolution and outperformed several state-of-the-art prediction methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication tones down the superlative "most complete" and emphasizes experimental testing details and comparative outperformance, which revises and narrows the original stronger completeness claim and presents it with lower confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep neural networks trained on simulated data can correctly infer the underlying dynamics of a cell response despite measurement noise and stochasticity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep neural networks trained on simulated data can correctly infer the underlying dynamics of a cell response despite measurement noise and stochasticity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result verbatim, with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The training set size and the amount of past data provided as inputs significantly affect the quality of predictions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The training set size and the amount of past data provided as inputs significantly affect the quality of predictions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this methodological result without substantive modification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An initial method for predicting a single trajectory was found to be ill-suited for multimodal dynamics in a bistable auto-activation circuit. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The published paper does not include the criticism of the initial single-trajectory method; instead it presents the updated approach, so this specific negative claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Updating the network architecture to predict the entire distribution of future states allows for accurate predictions of bimodal expression distributions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An updated network architecture can accurately predict bimodal expression distributions for multimodal dynamics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same technical result about the updated architecture's ability to predict bimodal distributions with equivalent confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methods developed can be applied to various prediction tasks necessary for controlling biological circuits in synthetic biology applications. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning methods are effective tools for predicting gene expression dynamics from time-series data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a broader, more strongly stated conclusion about the effectiveness of ML for predicting gene expression dynamics, expanding and strengthening the preprint's application-focused claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ProtGPT2 generates de novo protein sequences that follow the principles of natural ones. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ProtGPT2 generates de novo protein sequences that follow the principles of natural ones. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the central result identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 88% of ProtGPT2-generated proteins are globular, in line with natural sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: 88% of ProtGPT2-generated proteins are globular, which is consistent with natural sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The percentage and interpretation are preserved between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ProtGPT2 sequences are distantly related to natural ones and sample unexplored regions of protein space. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ProtGPT2 sequences are distantly related to natural ones, as shown by sensitive sequence searches in protein databases. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides specific evidence (sensitive searches) supporting the preprint's conclusion, clarifying how distant relationships were established while preserving the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AlphaFold prediction of ProtGPT2-sequences yielded well-folded non-idealized structures and revealed new topologies not captured in current structure databases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AlphaFold prediction of ProtGPT2-sequences yields well-folded non-idealized structures with topologies not captured in current structure databases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim mirrors the preprint's structural prediction results without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ProtGPT2 has the potential to generate de novo proteins in a high throughput fashion in a matter of seconds. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The final paper does not state the runtime/high-throughput potential claim present in the preprint, so this prospective performance claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model performance varies by drug, dataset, resistance metric, and species, reflecting the complexities of generating clinically relevant conclusions from machine learning-derived models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model performance varies by drug, dataset, resistance metric, and species. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same result about heterogeneity in model performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating relevant biological and epidemiological knowledge into model design and assessment can inform tailored modeling for individual drugs, pathogens, and clinical populations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating relevant biological and epidemiological knowledge into model design and assessment can inform tailored modeling for individual drugs, pathogens, and clinical populations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this methodological recommendation intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Continued comprehensive sampling and incorporation of up-to-date whole genome sequence data, resistance phenotypes, and treatment outcome data into model training will be crucial to the clinical utility and sustainability of machine learning-based molecular diagnostics. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Continued comprehensive sampling and incorporation of up-to-date whole genome sequence data, resistance phenotypes, and treatment outcome data into model training will be crucial to the clinical utility and sustainability of machine learning-based molecular diagnostics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same recommendation but expresses it with slightly lower stated certainty, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are significant biological and technical challenges facing the application of machine learning-based prediction of antibiotic resistance as a diagnostic tool. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The published version does not explicitly list this limitation as a standalone claim, so the specific phrasing of challenges was not carried forward.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ESMFold embeddings exhibit massive activations, with some channels having values 3000× higher than others, regardless of the input. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report this specific numerical/activation observation about ESMFold channels, so the claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ESMFold embeddings can be reduced by a factor of 128× along the channel and 8× along the length while retaining structure information at <2Å scale accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In continuous compression schemes, the ESMFold latent space can be reduced by factors of 128 along the channel and 8 along the length while retaining structure information at <2 Å scale accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same compression result with equivalent specificity and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A tokenized representation of all-atom structure that can be obtained from sequence alone has been constructed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tokenized representation of an all-atom structure can be obtained from the sequence alone while retaining high reconstruction accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the contribution as a high-accuracy reconstruction result, clarifying the quality and framing of the tokenized representation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CHEAP embeddings provide a compact representation of both protein structure and sequence and democratize representations captured by large models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CHEAP embeddings provide mechanistic interpretability insights and a flexible compressed representation of protein sequence and structure. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes mechanistic interpretability and frames the representation more strongly (higher certainty), adding interpretability as an explicit benefit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CHEAP embeddings are designed to have flexible downstream applications such as generation, search, and prediction. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CHEAP democratizes representations captured by large models and can enable flexible downstream applications such as generation, search, and prediction. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same intended downstream flexibility and democratization with comparable confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Inhibition of excitatory synaptic transmission in in vitro neural networks resulted in increases in both network burstiness and synchrony over time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inhibition of excitatory synaptic transmission in in vitro neural networks resulted in increases in both network burstiness and synchrony over time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental result is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The disruption in excitatory synaptic transmission during early network development likely affected inhibitory synaptic maturity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The disruption in excitatory synaptic transmission during early network development likely affected inhibitory synaptic maturity, leading to an overall decrease in network inhibition at later stages. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the inferred consequence of disrupted excitatory transmission by adding a downstream effect (overall decreased inhibition), clarifying and broadening the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings support the importance of excitatory-inhibitory balance in maintaining physiological bursting dynamics in neural networks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings support the importance of excitatory-inhibitory balance in maintaining physiological bursting dynamics and information processing capacity in neural networks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core conclusion while adding a related functional implication (information processing capacity), broadening the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The evolution of network bursts from physiological to potentially pathophysiological states is poorly understood. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The later paper does not include this explicit statement about gaps in understanding as a standalone claim, so it appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Unsupervised Global Vector for Word Representations embedding representations of administrative claims data combined with artificial neural network classification models significantly improves prediction of 30-day readmission. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Unsupervised Global Vector for Word Representations embedding representations of administrative claims data combined with artificial neural network classification models improves prediction of 30-day readmission. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with effectively identical wording and certainty; no substantive change in meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our best models increased the AUC for prediction of 30-day readmissions from 0.68 to 0.72 for AMI, 0.60 to 0.64 for HF, and 0.63 to 0.68 for PNA compared to hierarchical logistic regression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our best models increased the AUC for prediction of 30-day readmissions from 0.68 to 0.72 for AMI, 0.60 to 0.64 for HF, and 0.63 to 0.68 for PNA compared to hierarchical logistic regression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numeric values and comparison are identical between preprint and publication; claim meaning is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Risk-standardized hospital readmission rates calculated from our artificial neural network model that employed embeddings led to reclassification of approximately 10% of hospitals across categories of hospital performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Risk-standardized hospital readmission rates calculated from our artificial neural network model that employed embeddings led to reclassification of approximately 10% of hospitals across categories of hospital performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result claim with the same magnitude and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Prediction models that incorporate new methods classify hospitals differently than traditional regression-based approaches. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Prediction models that incorporate new methods classify hospitals differently than traditional regression-based approaches. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is carried forward verbatim into the publication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The role of new prediction models in assessing hospital performance warrants further investigation. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The role of new prediction models in assessing hospital performance warrants further investigation. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same tentative recommendation for further study with unchanged certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep neural networks can be used to map ECoG from speech production areas onto an intermediate representation of speech. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep neural networks can be used to map ECoG from speech production areas onto an intermediate representation of speech. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In a study with six participants, correlations up to r = 0.69 were achieved between the reconstructed and original logMel spectrograms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In a study with six participants, correlations up to r = 0.69 were achieved between the reconstructed and original logMel spectrograms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported quantitative result is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This is the first time that high-quality speech has been reconstructed from neural recordings during speech production using deep neural networks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This is the first time that high-quality speech has been reconstructed from neural recordings during speech production using deep neural networks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The novelty claim is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The PKPD model individualises warfarin dosing regimens with the highest success rate and efficiency, achieving 75.1% of individuals within the therapeutic range. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PKPD model individualises warfarin dosing regimens with the highest success rate and efficiency, achieving 75.1% of individuals within the therapeutic range. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the quantitative performance claim for the PKPD model verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The regression model and the deep RL model have success rates of 47.9% and 65.8%, respectively, with median time in the therapeutic range of 45% and 68%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The regression model and the deep RL model have success rates of 47.0% and 65.8%, respectively, with median time in the therapeutic range of 45% and 68%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adjusts the regression-model success rate slightly (47.9% → 47.0%), a minor numerical clarification/rounding that does not change the substantive comparison.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MIPD models can attain different degrees of individualisation, with the Regression model explaining variability only by covariates, while the Deep RL and PKPD models account for additional variation using monitoring data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MIPD models can attain different degrees of individualisation, with the Regression model explaining variability only by covariates, while the Deep RL and PKPD models account for additional variation using monitoring data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conceptual claim about degrees of individualisation is preserved unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Deep RL model focuses on control of the treatment response, whereas the PKPD model uses data to further individualisation of predictions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The specific comparative characterization of Deep RL vs PKPD present in the preprint does not appear in the publication; instead the publication adds a different limitation-oriented claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Crossover frequency varies along chromosomes and crossover interference limits the coincidence of closely spaced crossovers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Crossover frequency varies along chromosomes and crossover interference limits the coincidence of closely spaced crossovers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The biological observation is carried forward unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Crossovers can be measured by observing the inheritance of linked transgenes expressing different colors of fluorescent protein in Arabidopsis pollen tetrads. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepTetrad enables high-throughput measurements of crossover frequency and interference in individual plants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes that DeepTetrad enables high-throughput measurement of crossover properties but omits the preprint's specific mention of using linked multicolor transgenes; this is a loss of a specific methodological detail (clarification/generalization).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepTetrad is a deep learning-based image recognition package that enables high-throughput measurements of crossover frequency and interference in individual plants. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepTetrad is a deep learning-based image recognition package for pollen tetrad analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication splits/describes the contribution more succinctly (package for pollen tetrad analysis) while the preprint combined package and measurement capability; meaning is conserved but presentation is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepTetrad will accelerate genetic dissection of mechanisms that control meiotic recombination. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepTetrad will accelerate the genetic dissection of mechanisms that control meiotic recombination. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim about anticipated impact is retained without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The assay detected changes in marker expression in healthy platelets, at rest and on agonist activation, that are consistent with the literature. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The assay detected differences in marker expression in healthy platelets, at rest and on agonist activation, in both platelet-rich plasma and whole blood samples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds sample-context detail (platelet-rich plasma and whole blood), broadening the applicability while retaining the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning identified stimulated populations of platelets with high accuracy (>80%). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning identified stimulated populations of platelets with high accuracy (>80%). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This quantitative performance claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Differentiation between young and old platelet populations achieved 76% accuracy, primarily weighted by specific expression patterns. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning differentiation between young and old platelet populations achieved 76% accuracy, primarily weighted by forward scatter, CD41, side scatter, glycoprotein VI, CD61, and CD42b expression patterns. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the accuracy figure but names the specific features driving classification, clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings provide a novel assay to phenotype platelets coupled with a robust bioinformatics and machine learning workflow for deep analysis of the data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach provides a powerful phenotypic assay coupled with robust bioinformatic and machine learning workflows for deep analysis of platelet subpopulations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution as a 'powerful' approach and refines wording (phenotypic assay/workflows) without changing substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Coupling multi-parameter spectral flow cytometry with machine learning offers a powerful method to determine platelet sub-populations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach provides a powerful phenotypic assay coupled with robust bioinformatic and machine learning workflows for deep analysis of platelet subpopulations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same methodological conclusion but with stronger framing and integrates it into the stated approach; confidence in the claim is presented more strongly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning algorithms can rapidly develop predictive models for Alzheimer's disease and related dementias using large datasets. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning algorithms can rapidly develop predictive models for ADRD with massive datasets, without requiring hypothesis-driven feature engineering. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same point about rapid model development with large datasets but adds the detail about not requiring hypothesis-driven feature engineering, so the claim is clarified rather than changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Lasso algorithm selected a 50-variable model with an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.693 for predicting Alzheimer's disease and related dementias. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine learning model developed using a national de-identified dataset achieved an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.693 for predicting ADRD 4-5 years in advance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same AUC result and adds the prediction horizon (4–5 years); this adds detail to the preprint result (clarification) though some Lasso-specific wording is split across publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model developed using machine learning compares favorably with historical attempts at modeling traditional forms of data for predicting dementia. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or emphasize any direct comparison claiming that the model compares favorably to historical attempts, so this preprint conclusion is not present in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Top diagnosis codes in the model included memory loss, Parkinson’s disease, mild cognitive impairment, and bipolar disorder. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Lasso algorithm selected a 50-variable model for predicting ADRD, which included top diagnosis codes such as memory loss and Parkinson's disease. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same top diagnosis codes but lists a subset (memory loss, Parkinson's) and omits MCI and bipolar disorder, which represents a narrowing/revision of the specific reported top codes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models like the one developed could identify populations at higher risk for near-term diagnosis of dementia for further intervention or research. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning algorithms using administrative claims data can represent a novel approach to predicting Alzheimer's disease and related dementias (ADRD). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the approach as a novel predictive method (supporting identification of at-risk populations) but does not explicitly state the intervention/research use; the claim is therefore clarified and presented with stronger certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High out-of-distribution performance can be achieved when the negative dataset contains more similar samples to the positive dataset despite a lower within-distribution performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: High out-of-distribution performance can be achieved when the negative dataset contains more similar samples to the positive dataset, despite lower in-distribution performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with essentially identical wording and implications.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Binding rules discovered as associated with positive data change based on the negative data used. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Binding rules associated with positive data change based on the negative data used. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This is the same empirical observation reported in both versions with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work underscores the role of dataset composition, including negative data selection, in creating robust, generalizable, and biology-aware sequence-based ML models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work underscores the role of dataset composition in creating robust, generalizable and biology-aware sequence-based ML models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same central conclusion about dataset composition and model robustness but reports it with lower stated certainty (tempered confidence).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: sc-Long is a billion-parameter foundation model pretrained on 48 million cells that captures long-range dependencies between all genes in the human genome. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: scLong is a billion-parameter foundation model pretrained on 48 million cells that captures long-range dependencies between all genes in the human genome. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution claim verbatim (name formatting aside) with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: sc-Long integrates gene knowledge from the Gene Ontology using a graph convolutional network, enhancing its understanding of gene functions and relationships. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: scLong integrates gene knowledge from the Gene Ontology using a graph convolutional network, enhancing its understanding of gene functions and relationships. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same description of GO integration via a GCN; the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In extensive evaluations, sc-Long surpasses both state-of-the-art scRNA-seq foundation models and task-specific models across diverse tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In extensive evaluations, scLong surpasses both state-of-the-art scRNA-seq foundation models and task-specific models across diverse tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported evaluation results are carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing approaches to scRNA-seq data analysis are limited to modeling a small subset of highly expressed genes and do not integrate external gene-specific knowledge. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: scLong enables the analysis of lowly expressed genes, which are often excluded by existing foundation models but play critical roles in cellular processes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint framed this content as a limitation of existing approaches; the publication reframes it positively as a capability of scLong (enabling lowly expressed gene analysis), which is a substantive revision and broadens the implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Increased GABA levels during the uncertainty condition suggest recruitment of inhibitory systems during high cognitive load when trying to learn. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Increased GABA levels were found during the uncertainty condition, potentially reflecting recruitment of inhibitory systems during high cognitive load. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same observation but frames it with stronger certainty; the substantive interpretation is the same, so unchanged with amplified confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Higher GABA levels during the null (baseline) condition correlated with improved discrimination learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Higher GABA levels during the null (baseline) condition correlated with improved discrimination learning. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is the same, but the publication reports it with lower stated certainty compared to the preprint (tempered confidence).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Excitatory and inhibitory levels were correlated during high cognitive load. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Glutamate and GABA levels were correlated during high cognitive load. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the excitatory and inhibitory measures (glutamate and GABA) but conveys the same correlation finding without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The availability of dACC inhibitory resources enables successful learning. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The availability of dACC inhibitory resources enables successful learning. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is preserved verbatim, but the publication down-weights the certainty of this interpretive claim (tempered confidence).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach establishes a novel way to examine the contribution of the balance between excitation and inhibition to learning and motivation in behaving humans. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include an explicit claim about establishing a novel methodological approach to examine E/I balance in behaving humans, so this contribution claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study introduces a novel framework leveraging SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) to identify crucial Functional Network Connectivity (FNC) features that distinguish between two distinct population classes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel framework leveraging SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAPs) identifies crucial FNC features distinguishing between two distinct population classes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution claim with equivalent wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework achieves an accuracy of 81.04% in distinguishing between controls and schizophrenia patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework achieves an accuracy of 81.04% in distinguishing between controls and schizophrenia (SZ) patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported classification performance is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework also achieves an accuracy of 71.38% in distinguishing between middle-aged adults and older adults. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework achieves an accuracy of 71.38% in distinguishing between middle aged adults and old aged adults. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This performance claim is repeated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis underscores the pivotal role of the cognitive control network (CCN), subcortical network (SCN), and somatomotor network (SMN) in discerning individuals with schizophrenia from controls. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The cognitive control network (CCN), subcortical network (SCN), and somatomotor network play a pivotal role in discerning individuals with SZ from controls. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The networks identified are the same, but the publication states this conclusion with higher certainty (amplified confidence).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The platform identifies CCN and SCN as the most important networks separating young adults from older adults. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The platform identifies CCN and SCN as the most important networks separating young adults from older individuals. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The specific networks and comparative groups are the same; the publication presents the conclusion with stronger certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individual differences in lose-lose slowing relate to information uncertainty due to sampling.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individual differences in lose-lose slowing relate to information uncertainty due to sampling.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result verbatim; meaning, scope and confidence are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Less efficient response inhibition in the stop-signal task is associated with lose-lose slowing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Less efficient response inhibition in the stop-signal task is associated with lose-lose slowing.
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over without substantive change in content or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Subthalamic nucleus (STN) BOLD activity is related to variability in stopping latencies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Subthalamic nucleus (STN) BOLD activity relates to variability in stopping latencies.
alignments.rationale: Wording is slightly adjusted but the relationship and confidence remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Weaker fronto-subthalamic connectivity is related to slowing and information sampling.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Weaker frontosubthalamic connectivity is related to slowing and information sampling.
alignments.rationale: Content is the same but the publication downgrades certainty from high to medium, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Slowing due to decision uncertainty could result from an inefficient release of the 'hold your horses' mechanism.
alignments.rationale: This is a new interpretive/concluding statement in the publication that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We present 11 merged microarray-acquired datasets (MMDs) of major cancer types, curating 8,386 patient-derived tumor and tumor-free samples from 95 GEO datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Merged microarray-acquired datasets (MMDs) were curated from 8,386 patient-derived tumor and tumor-free samples across 11 major cancer types.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the dataset curation contribution with essentially the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Highly concordant MMD-derived patterns of genome-wide differential gene expression were observed with matching TCGA cohorts.
alignments.rationale: The specific claim about concordant genome-wide differential-expression patterns is not explicitly present in the publication's set of claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clinical models trained from all MMDs, except breast MMD, can be directly applied to RNA-seq-acquired TCGA data with an average accuracy of 0.96 in classifying cancer.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Diagnostic models trained from MMDs can be directly applied to RNA-seq-acquired TCGA data with high classification accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core result but omits the specific numeric accuracy and the breast exception, generalizing the claim to high accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning optimized MMD further aids to reveal immune landscape of human cancers critically needed in disease management and clinical interventions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning optimized MMD aids in revealing the immune landscape across various carcinomas, which is critically needed in disease management and clinical interventions.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same conclusion about ML-optimized MMDs aiding immune-landscape analysis with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This single data source may serve as an excellent training or test set to apply, develop, and refine machine learning algorithms that can be tapped to better define genomic landscape of human cancers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The unified data source may serve as an excellent training or test set for developing and refining machine learning algorithms to better define the genomic landscape of human cancers.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this concluding recommendation with minimal wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Increased synaptic serotonin reduces sensitivity specifically for outcomes in aversive contexts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Increasing synaptic serotonin reduces sensitivity for outcomes in aversive contexts.
alignments.rationale: The result is restated directly with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Increasing synaptic serotonin enhances behavioural inhibition and shifts bias towards impulse control during exposure to aversive emotional probes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Increasing synaptic serotonin enhances behavioural inhibition and shifts bias towards impulse control during exposure to aversive emotional probes.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the behavioural-inhibition finding verbatim; no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effects of increased synaptic serotonin are associated with overall improvements in memory for neutral verbal information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Overall improvements in memory for neutral verbal information are observed with increased synaptic serotonin.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents this memory result with higher stated certainty (medium -> high), so confidence is amplified though the content is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings highlight the critical role of increased synaptic serotonin in guiding decision-making within aversive and neutral contexts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings highlight the direct effects of increasing synaptic serotonin on human behaviour, underlining its role in guiding decision-making within aversive and more neutral contexts.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the conclusion to emphasize direct effects on behaviour but lowers overall stated certainty (high -> medium), so it's a clarification with tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A reinforcement learning agent can forget a large percentage of older memories without any performance impairments when using mnemonic representations that contain structural information about space.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A reinforcement learning agent can forget a large percentage of older memories without any performance impairments when using mnemonic representations that contain structural information about space.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this core result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Forgetting can provide a benefit in performance compared to agents with unbounded memories.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Forgetting can provide a benefit in performance compared to agents with unbounded memories.
alignments.rationale: The claim is preserved verbatim with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Forgetting reduces the influence of outdated information and infrequently visited states on the policies produced by the episodic control system.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Forgetting reduces the influence of outdated information and infrequently visited states on the policies produced by the episodic control system.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same mechanistic result as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Some degree of forgetting can be beneficial for decision making, supporting the hypothesis that the brain forgets more than is required by capacity limitations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Some degree of forgetting can be beneficial for decision making, helping to explain why the brain forgets more than is required by capacity limitations.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is reworded slightly but preserves the same interpretation and level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dopamine transients, when constrained within appropriate learning situations, support valueless associative learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dopamine transients support associative learning rather than model-free learning in situations appropriate for the appearance of a prediction error.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the claim to contrast associative vs model-free learning and links it to prediction-error contexts, narrowing and clarifying the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Antecedent cues failed to acquire value during the experiments conducted with optogenetically activated dopamine neurons.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Antecedent cues failed to acquire value and instead entered into associations with later events during the experiments.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core result but explicitly states that cues formed associations rather than value, clarifying the nature of the learning observed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Causal optogenetic studies suggest that artificially induced dopamine transients can cause lasting changes in behavior.
alignments.rationale: The specific conclusion about lasting behavioral changes from artificial dopamine transients is not represented among the publication claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study addresses a gap in previous research by assessing learning under conditions where an endogenous prediction error would occur.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes an added contribution-focused statement about addressing a gap in prior work that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The optimal set of 370 features for effector prediction was determined through analysis of disparate predictive features used in existing machine learning algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our optimal features for effector prediction are quite robust as indicated by the good results from all of our models.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same core claim about an identified optimal feature set but shifts emphasis to robustness as evidenced by model results, reducing methodological detail — thus a clarification rather than an unchanged claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model 1, which uses all 370 features with a support vector machine, achieved slightly better accuracy than the other models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model 1, which uses all 370 features with a support vector machine, has slightly better accuracy compared to other models.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result with essentially identical wording and emphasis, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model 1 predicted 760 effector proteins, which is more than any other study, with 315 of those being validated.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model 1 predicted 472 effector proteins that are deemed highly probable to be effectors and include 94% of known effectors.
alignments.rationale: The published numbers and framing differ substantially (760->472, validation statistics replaced by percent coverage of known effectors), representing a substantive revision and a narrowed, more conservative result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results of the three models developed in this study agree well with those of other researchers, whose models predicted only 126 and 311 candidate effectors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results of our three models agree well with those of other researchers, whose models only predicted 126 and 311 candidate effectors.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same comparative result with very similar wording and confidence, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The FUSION workflow efficiently separated traces that passed quality filters from unusable ones.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The FUSION workflow efficiently separated traces that passed quality filters from unusable ones.
alignments.rationale: Identical statement in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FUSION classified curves with high accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FUSION classified curves with high accuracy.
alignments.rationale: Identical result reported in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FUSION identified unfolding pathways undetected by the user.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FUSION identified unfolding pathways that were undetected by the user.
alignments.rationale: Claim is retained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study demonstrates the potential of machine learning to accelerate data analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study demonstrates the potential of machine learning to accelerate data analysis and generate new insights in protein biophysics.
alignments.rationale: The publication combines this claim with an allied claim about generating new insights but preserves the original point about accelerating data analysis; mapping is direct.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study demonstrates the potential of machine learning to generate new insights in protein biophysics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study demonstrates the potential of machine learning to accelerate data analysis and generate new insights in protein biophysics.
alignments.rationale: The publication merges this conclusion with the acceleration claim but retains the same substantive content about generating new biophysical insights.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A gradient boosting model (GBM) showed better predictive performance compared with a linear regression model (LRM) and a random forest model (RFM) in terms of relative bias and relative root mean square error for recruitment prediction in the last 5 years.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A gradient boosting model (GBM) showed better predictive performance for recruitment prediction compared to other models.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core result that GBM outperforms other models but omits the specific comparators, metrics, and time window, so the claim is a clarified (less detailed) version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The most influential feature for GBM was spawning stock biomass in the last year, followed by the fishing rate for older fish.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The most influential feature for the GBM was spawning stock biomass in the last year.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms spawning stock biomass as the top predictor but drops the secondary importance of older-fish fishing rate, narrowing the scope of the original feature-ranking claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sea surface temperature (SST) was a very weak predictor in the GBM but was the most influential feature in the LRM.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sea temperatures at various depths were unimportant predictors in the GBM model.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the GBM part (temperatures unimportant) but removes the comparison to LRM and expands to temperatures at depths; this narrows and slightly reframes the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The difference among models suggests the importance of nonlinearity and variable interactions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The difference in important predictors among models suggests the importance of nonlinearity and incorporating multiple variables simultaneously.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the original inference about nonlinearity and adds an explicit emphasis on simultaneous incorporation of multiple variables, broadening the interpretive scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning methods will greatly contribute to fish recruitment forecast and thereby sustainable fisheries management.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study highlights the potential usefulness of GBM for fish recruitment forecast and sustainable fisheries management.
alignments.rationale: The preprint makes a broad, strong claim about machine learning; the publication tempers and narrows this to the usefulness of GBM specifically with lower certainty, so the claim is revised and tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The curation of data with informative features requires both expertise in machine learning and a strong biological and ecotoxicological background.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The curation of data with informative features in machine learning for ecotoxicology requires expertise in both machine learning and biology.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same limitation, preserving the core requirement for combined expertise; wording is slightly condensed but meaning is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model performances can only be compared across studies when the same dataset, cleaning, and splittings were used.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model performances in ecotoxicological studies can only be compared when the same dataset, cleaning, and splittings are used.
alignments.rationale: The published claim is the same but presented with a lower stated certainty (medium), so content is unchanged but expressed with tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ADORE is an extensive and well-described dataset on acute aquatic toxicity in three relevant taxonomic groups.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ADORE is an extensive and well-described dataset on acute aquatic toxicity that includes data on fish, crustaceans, and algae.
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the three taxonomic groups explicitly (fish, crustaceans, algae), adding specificity to the original dataset description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset includes phylogenetic and species-specific data on the species as well as chemical properties and molecular representations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset ADORE includes phylogenetic and species-specific data as well as chemical properties and molecular representations.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution verbatim; the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The authors propose specific relevant challenges on subsets of the data to encourage researchers to achieve the best model performances.
alignments.rationale: This proposal/initiative mentioned in the preprint does not appear among the published claims, indicating it was removed or not retained in the final publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) can classify tumor/normal status of whole slide images in 19 cancer types with consistently high AUCs (0.995±0.008).
alignments.pub_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) can classify tumor/normal status of whole slide images (WSIs) in 19 cancer types with consistently high AUCs (0.995 ± 0.008).
alignments.rationale: The result is reported identically in the publication, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Tumor/normal CNNs trained on one tissue are effective in others, achieving an AUC of 0.88±0.11.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CNNs trained on one tissue type are effective in classifying tumor/normal status in other tissue types, achieving an AUC of 0.88 ± 0.11.
alignments.rationale: Same cross-tissue performance claim appears in the publication with essentially identical numbers and wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Breast cancers, bladder cancers, and uterine cancers have spatial patterns that are particularly easy to detect, suggesting these cancers can be canonical types for image analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Breast cancers, bladder cancers, and uterine cancers exhibit spatial patterns that are particularly easy to detect, suggesting they can be canonical types for image analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion but reports a lower certainty (publication labels it low), so the claim content is unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patterns for TP53 mutations can be detected, with WSI self- and cross-tissue AUCs ranging from 0.65-0.80.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patterns for TP53 mutations can be detected with WSI self- and cross-tissue AUCs ranging from 0.65-0.80.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same mutation-detection performance with the same AUC range, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results demonstrate the power of CNNs for histopathological classification and for cross-comparisons to reveal conserved spatial biology.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Classifier comparisons reveal intra-slide spatial similarities, with an average tile-level correlation of 0.45 ± 0.16 between classifier pairs.
alignments.rationale: The broad preprint conclusion about demonstrating power and revealing conserved spatial biology is refined in the publication into a specific measurable result (tile-level correlation), narrowing the scope and clarifying the evidence supporting the conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep spectral learning (DSL) approach for oximetry provides uncertainty quantification for each sO 2 prediction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep spectral learning (DSL) approach provides uncertainty quantification for each sO2 prediction. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same capability (uncertainty quantification per sO2 prediction) with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DSL predictions show significantly lower mean-square errors compared to the traditional least-squares fitting (LSF) method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DSL-predicted sO2 shows significantly lower mean-square errors than those of the traditional least-squares fitting (LSF) method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same comparative result (lower MSE vs LSF) with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DSL is highly robust to experimental variations and adaptive to depth-dependent backscattering spectra. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DSL demonstrates robustness and generalizability across two separate in vivo experiments on rat retinas. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies robustness in the concrete context of two in vivo rat experiments, narrowing the preprint's broader statement about experimental variations and adaptivity to a defined experimental demonstration.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For the first time, en face maps of retinal oximetry along with pixel-wise confidence assessment have been demonstrated using DSL. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: For the first time, en face maps of retinal oximetry along with a pixel-wise confidence assessment have been demonstrated. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the novel demonstration (en face oximetry maps with pixel-wise confidence) verbatim and attributes the same novelty and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepQSM can solve the ill-posed field-to-source inversion on real-world in vivo MRI phase data without the need for manual parameter tuning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepQSM is capable of solving the ill-posed field-to-source inversion problem using in vivo MRI phase data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the core inversion capability on in vivo data but omits the explicit claim about operating without manual parameter tuning, so the claim is slightly revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepQSM’s susceptibility maps enable identification of deep brain substructures that are not visible in MRI phase data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The magnetic susceptibility maps reconstructed by DeepQSM enable the identification of deep brain substructures and provide information on their respective magnetic tissue properties. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same ability (identifying deep brain substructures) and extends it slightly by noting tissue property information, but the core claim remains intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepQSM shows sensitivity to white matter lesions in patients with multiple sclerosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or assert sensitivity to white matter lesions or specific clinical findings in MS patients, so this preprint claim is not reflected in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepQSM can determine the composition of myelin sheets of nerve fibers in the brain and assess quantitative information on iron homeostasis and its dysregulation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantitative susceptibility mapping (QSM) provides relevant information on biological tissue properties, including myelin, iron, and calcium in vivo. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes a more general statement about QSM providing information on myelin and iron in vivo, rather than the preprint's stronger claim that DeepQSM can determine specific myelin composition and iron homeostasis, so the claim is toned down and generalized.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LOCA-ULM outperformed conventional localization with superior MB detection accuracy of 94.0% compared to 74.9%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LOCA-ULM enhanced microbubble detection accuracy to 97.8% and reduced the missing rate to 23.8%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions claim substantially improved detection accuracy over conventional localization, but the publication reports different (updated) numeric results, so the claim is revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LOCA-ULM achieved a significantly lower MB missing rate of 13.2% compared to 74.8% in conventional localization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LOCA-ULM enhanced microbubble detection accuracy to 97.8% and reduced the missing rate to 23.8%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim of a reduced missing rate but reports a different (less extreme) missing-rate value, indicating a revision of the numerical result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In vivo, LOCA-ULM achieved up to a three-fold increase in MB localization efficiency compared to conventional ULM. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In in vivo rat brain imaging, LOCA-ULM revealed dense cerebrovascular networks and spatially adjacent microvessels undetected by conventional ULM. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes qualitative in vivo gains (revealing dense networks and adjacent microvessels) rather than reporting a specific 'three-fold' localization-efficiency factor, shifting the emphasis from a numeric efficiency metric to detection capability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LOCA-ULM resulted in a × 9.5 faster vessel saturation rate than conventional ULM. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LOCA-ULM significantly increased the functional imaging sensitivity of fULM to hemodynamic responses invoked by whisker stimulations in the rat brain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports increased functional imaging sensitivity (a related performance improvement) but does not mention the specific '×9.5 faster vessel saturation' metric, so the original numeric claim was revised into a different performance measure.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Helixer provides a fully applicable, fast, and user-friendly tool for predicting primary gene models from DNA sequence alone. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Helixer operates without requiring additional experimental data such as RNA sequencing, making it broadly applicable to diverse species. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the core claim that Helixer works from sequence alone (no extra experimental data), emphasizing broad applicability across species; this clarifies and broadens the preprint's applicability claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The quality of Helixer's predictions is state-of-the-art, scoring closer to references than predictions from other de novo tools. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Helixer’s pretrained models achieve accuracy on par with or exceeding current tools, producing gene annotations that closely match expert-curated references across multiple evaluation metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance claim (accuracy on par with or exceeding current tools and close to references) using equivalent metrics and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Helixer's predictions can be used independently or integrated into existing pipelines to enhance quality further. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Helixer enables immediate use on genomes without retraining, providing an efficient, accessible solution for genome annotation in both research and applied settings. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes immediate use without retraining, which clarifies and strengthens the practical-use aspect of the preprint claim about integration and independent use.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is substantial potential for further improvements and advancements in gene calling with Deep Learning. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The published claims focus on Helixer's current capabilities and do not explicitly discuss the broader potential for future improvements using deep learning, so this forward-looking preprint statement is not present in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two types of highly accurate deep convolutional neural networks (CNNs) are a powerful strategy to uncover the sequence determinants of various histone modification patterns in different cell types. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The cell models can accurately predict the histone marks in each cell type. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes accurate prediction of histone marks rather than the methodological assertion that two CNN types are a strategy to 'uncover' sequence determinants, shifting the emphasis from discovery strategy to predictive performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sequence motifs learned by the CNN models are highly similar to known binding motifs of transcription factors that play important roles in CD4+ T cell differentiation. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using these models, we can predict the importance of the learned motifs and their interactions in determining specific histone marker patterns in the cell types. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not state that the models can predict motif importance and interactions explicitly, so this predictive-interpretation claim from the preprint is not present in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Epiphany demonstrates robust performance and generalization to held-out chromosomes within and across cell types and species. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predicted contact matrices from Epiphany yield accurate TAD and significant interaction calls. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: CAMEL outperformed contending approaches without using molecular read-outs from unseen conditions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: CAMEL provides a valuable tool for using protein allocation in biotechnological applications. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results published in Poore and Kopylova et al. 2020 revealed the possibility of being able to almost perfectly differentiate between types of tumour based on their microbial composition using machine learning models. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: There are concerns regarding the contribution of contamination, handling of batch effects, and false positive classifications in the study. (certainty=high, type=limitation)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of TACIT revealed dozens of enhancer-phenotype associations, including those related to brain size and conditions like microcephaly or macrocephaly. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieved a test set F1 score of 0.9351 in predicting β1,6GlcNAc-branching across T cell subtypes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieved a test set F1 score of 0.9351 in predicting β1,6GlcNAc-branching across T cell subtypes. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model interpretation via SHAP identified highly predictive genes that impact branched glycan levels and their biology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model interpretation via SHAP identified highly predictive genes that impact branched glycan levels and their biology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the SHAP-based interpretation claim and its implications.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identified predictive genes included low-abundance genes not captured by conventional differential expression analysis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same conclusion but expresses it with slightly more measured certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed exemplar guided graph network (EGGN) accurately predicts gene expression from each window of a tissue slide image. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework includes a graph construction strategy that connects windows and exemplars for exemplar learning in gene expression prediction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Exemplar learning is applied to dynamically boost gene expression prediction from nearest/similar exemplars of a given tissue slide image window. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A graph exemplar bridging block is introduced to revise window features using their nearest exemplars. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Exemplar learning is applied to dynamically boost gene expression prediction from nearest/similar exemplars of a given tissue slide image window. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the exemplar-based revision idea but omits the specific 'graph exemplar bridging block' implementation and lowers certainty, so the claim is clarified and slightly tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments on standard benchmark datasets demonstrate the superiority of the proposed approach compared to past state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments on standard benchmark datasets indicate the superiority of the EGGN approach when compared with past state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pareto MTL can obtain a better Pareto front than the naive scalarization approach typically encountered in the literature. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pareto multi-task learning (Pareto MTL) can obtain a better Pareto front than the naive scalarization approach typically encountered in the literature. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Applying a neural-network based estimator called Mutual Information Neural Estimation (MINE) shows benefits over the more standard Maximum Mean Discrepancy (MMD) measure. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Applying Mutual Information Neural Estimation (MINE) to measure batch effect shows benefits over the more standard maximum mean discrepancy measure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same comparative claim but states it with higher certainty, so the claim content is unchanged but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Pareto front between conservation of biological variation and batch effect removal is a valuable tool for researchers in computational biology. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Pareto front between conservation of biological variation and batch effect removal is a valuable tool for researchers in computational biology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
