alignments.preprint_claim_text: The electronic nose can differentiate Rhodnius pallensces naturally infected with T. cruzi with less than 1% of error in classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The electronic nose differentiated Rhodnius pallensces naturally infected with T. cruzi with less than 1% error in classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result and error rate are the same; wording change (tense) does not alter meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The designed electronic nose can detect particular smelling footprints of insects. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The designed device can detect particular smelling footprints of insects. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same capability (device detects insect odor footprints) with synonymous wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The electronic nose could be a tool to discriminate against insects in the future. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An electronic nose like the one designed could be a tool to discriminate against insects in the future. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The speculative future utility claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: scaLR offers comparable prediction accuracy and requires less model training time and compute resources than existing Python-based pipelines and frameworks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: scaLR offers comparable prediction accuracy and requires less model training time and computational resources than existing Python-based pipelines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the resource/accuracy comparison but narrows the comparator language from "pipelines and frameworks" to "pipelines," a modest narrowing/clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: scaLR efficiently handles large sample datasets ( > 11.4 million cells) with minimal resource usage (29GB RAM, 12GB GPU, and 8 CPUs) while maintaining high prediction accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: scaLR efficiently processes data into feature subsets and samples in batches to reduce the required memory for processing large datasets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the batching/subsetting approach rather than the explicit large-dataset capacity and exact resource numbers reported in the preprint, changing the claim focus and narrowing quantified capacity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: scaLR is capable of ranking the biomarker association with specific cell types and diseases. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel feature extraction algorithm in scaLR selects the top-K features based on their importance after training on feature subsets. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the capability as an algorithmic feature-selection mechanism (top-K by importance) rather than an explicit claim about ranking biomarker associations to cell types/diseases, narrowing the biological interpretation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: scaLR is engineered to utilize minimal computational resources while maintaining comparable execution times to existing frameworks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: scaLR offers comparable prediction accuracy and requires less model training time and computational resources than existing Python-based pipelines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication articulates the same conclusion about resource efficiency and comparable performance; wording emphasizes pipelines and frames as a result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Prop3D is a platform that allows for the creation, sharing, and extensible reuse of libraries of protein domains with various biophysical and evolutionary properties. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Prop3D is a platform that allows for the creation, sharing, and extensible reuse of libraries of protein domains featurized with biophysical and evolutionary properties. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the platform's purpose with synonymous wording (featurized vs various properties) without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Prop3D-20sf dataset provides a hand-crafted, already-featurized dataset of protein domains for 20 highly-populated CATH families. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Prop3D-20sf dataset provides a hand-crafted, already-featurized dataset of protein domains for 20 highly-populated CATH families. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The dataset description is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Prop3D framework can be customized and deployed on various cloud-based compute platforms, achieving scalability through distributed HDF5 files via HSDS. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Prop3D framework can be customized and deployed on various cloud-based compute platforms, achieving scalability by saving results to distributed HDF5 files via HSDS. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the mechanism for scalability (saving results to distributed HDF5 via HSDS) but does not change the substantive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The construction of Prop3D-20sf explicitly considers the issue of 'data leakage' arising from the evolutionary relationships between proteins. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Prop3D-20sf’s construction explicitly takes into account the enigma of ‘data leakage’ stemming from the evolutionary relationships between proteins. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same concern and mitigation about data leakage due to evolutionary relationships with only stylistic change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LncRNAs are involved in many regulatory roles inside the cell, and over 95% of known lncRNAs have yet to have their functionality established.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Long non-coding RNAs (lncRNAs) serve as vital regulators of cellular processes and potential disease biomarkers.
alignments.rationale: Both claims assert lncRNAs' regulatory importance; the publication frames them as vital regulators/biomarkers and omits the specific '95% unknown' statistic, narrowing and clarifying the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current in silico methods for lncRNA prediction are limited in accuracy and robustness due to their reliance on primary-sequence based features.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Annotating lncRNA for newly sequenced species is challenging due to inconsistencies in functional annotations and limitations of reference-based methods.
alignments.rationale: Both describe limitations of current approaches; the publication frames the limitation specifically for newly sequenced species and reference-based methods, narrowing the original, broader critique.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate that comprehensive functional annotation of lncRNAs can be achieved by collating their interactome and secondary structure information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrating secondary structure and interactome information, in addition to using transcript sequence, enables a comprehensive functional annotation of lncRNAs.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core claim but explicitly notes inclusion of transcript sequence as well, clarifying rather than changing the conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our machine learning models exceed the performance of state-of-the-art methods with AUC values of over 0.9 for a diverse collection of species.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the specific high-performance AUC claim or cross-species performance; that specific performance assertion appears omitted in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We built a pipeline called linc2function that provides the necessary information to functionally annotate a lncRNA conveniently in a single window.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We developed a pipeline for identifying and annotating transcript sequences at the isoform level.
alignments.rationale: Both describe development of a pipeline; the publication emphasizes isoform-level identification/annotation, which narrows and clarifies the original pipeline description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sex differences in aortic valve stenosis progression depend on valvular interstitial cells activating to myofibroblasts.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims focus on sex-specific drug responses and frameworks rather than the specific mechanistic result about VIC activation; this mechanistic claim is not present in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results reveal that anti-fibrotic drug efficacy and combinatorial interactions are dependent on cell sex.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sex-specific drug response models can be used to predict sex biases in drug efficacy and combinatorial interactions.
alignments.rationale: Both claims assert that drug efficacy and combinatorial interactions vary by sex; the publication reframes this in terms of predictive models but retains the core result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study provides a framework for developing clinically relevant aortic valve stenosis treatment strategies through the integration of high-throughput hydrogel cell culture platforms and AI-driven drug optimization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides a framework for developing AVS treatments through the integration of high-throughput hydrogel cell culture platforms and AI-driven drug optimization.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same framework claim with higher stated certainty; content and scope are consistent but confidence is stronger in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The workflow towards designing targeted aortic valve stenosis drug combinations may help accelerate drug development for male and female patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Designing targeted AVS drug combinations may help accelerate AVS drug development and address health disparities in AVS treatment outcomes.
alignments.rationale: Both claims state that designing targeted combinations may accelerate development; the publication clarifies by adding the goal of addressing health disparities, broadening the societal scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Constitutive artificial neural networks can effectively capture the complex stress-strain relationships of the pulmonary artery.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Constitutive artificial neural networks can effectively capture the complex stress–strain relationships of the pulmonary artery.
alignments.rationale: Claims are effectively identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identifies an isotropic exponential first-invariant term and an anisotropic quadratic fifth-invariant term in the material behavior of the pulmonary artery.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identifies an isotropic exponential first-invariant term and an anisotropic quadratic fifth-invariant term in the material behavior of the pulmonary artery.
alignments.rationale: Claims match verbatim in substance and specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Constitutive models incorporating both identified terms can reliably predict arterial responses under various loading conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Constitutive models incorporating both identified terms can reliably predict arterial responses under diverse loading conditions.
alignments.rationale: Same claim restated with synonymous wording; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model outcomes enhance the understanding of the biomechanical properties of pulmonary tissue and can improve surgical outcomes after the Ross procedure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model outcomes enhance the understanding of the biomechanical properties of pulmonary tissue and can improve surgical outcomes after the Ross procedure.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this conclusion unchanged from the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ASEM pipeline successfully automates the identification of various intracellular structures using a convolutional neural network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Automated segmentation of intracellular substructures in electron microscopy (ASEM) is a new pipeline that trains a convolutional neural network to detect structures of varying sizes and complexities.
alignments.rationale: Both statements describe the ASEM pipeline and its CNN-based automated detection of intracellular structures; content is consistent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ASEM allows for the detection of structures of a wide range in size and complexity with only a small number of sparsely annotated ground truth annotations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dedicated models for each structure can be obtained based on a small number of sparsely annotated ground truth images from only one or two cells.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies 'one or two cells' and per-structure dedicated models, clarifying and narrowing the original, more general claim about sparse annotations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model generalization to different imaging conditions is improved by refining an already trained model with a few additional annotations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model generalization is improved by a rapid, computationally effective strategy that includes a few additional annotations.
alignments.rationale: Both describe the same strategy of improving generalization via a small number of additional annotations; wording differs but meaning is consistent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ASEM enabled the uncovering of a wide range of membrane-nuclear pore diameters within a single cell.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A wide range of membrane-nuclear pore diameters was uncovered within a single cell.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this specific result directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Morphological metrics derived from clathrin coated pits and vesicles at all stages of maturation are consistent with the classical constant-growth assembly model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Morphological metrics derived from clathrin-coated pits and vesicles are consistent with the classical constant-growth assembly model.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the conclusion about morphological metrics supporting the classical model with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fabrication of patterned substrates for neuronal culture with controlled structure at the nano- and microscale promotes the selective adhesion of neuronal cells.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel approach using supersonic cluster beam deposition of zirconia nanoparticles allows for the patterning of small nanostructured cell-adhesive areas on nonadhesive antifouling glass surfaces.
alignments.rationale: Both claims assert patterned nanostructured adhesive areas promote selective adhesion; the publication names the fabrication method, clarifying the approach.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nanostructured micrometric dots patterned on glass substrates can direct the maturation of neural networks through mechanotransductive signaling.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integrated nanostructured surfaces possess extracellular matrix-like nanotopographies that promote neuronal differentiation due to their impact on mechanotransductive processes.
alignments.rationale: Both link nanotopography, mechanotransduction, and neuronal development; the publication frames the outcome as promotion of differentiation and explicitly links it to ECM-like nanotopographies, clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PC12 cells and cultured hippocampal neurons adhere only to the nanostructured zirconia regions of the micropatterned substrates.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both neuron-like PC12 cells and primary cultures of rat hippocampal neurons specifically adhered to the nanostructured regions after initial uniform plating.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the specific adhesion result for PC12 and hippocampal neurons with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The role of mechanotransductive signaling in the growth and organization of neural networks has not been previously investigated.
alignments.rationale: The publication discusses mechanotransductive impacts and reports results, so the preprint's prior-unknown limitation/assertion is not maintained in the published claims and appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A systematic method for extracting essential genome editing information using large language models has been developed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A systematic method for extracting essential genome editing information using large language models has been developed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim, so the claim is unchanged in meaning, scope, and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method allows for a systematic and efficient investigation of genome editing information that cannot be achieved using the current Genome Editing Meta-database alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method allows for a systematic and efficient investigation of genome editing information that cannot be achieved using the current Genome Editing Meta-database alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result claim with the same scope and certainty, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The extracted genome editing information and novel genome editing-related scores are expected to facilitate the efficient selection of target genes for genome editing. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The extracted genome editing information and novel genome editing-related scores are expected to facilitate the efficient selection of target genes for genome editing. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same expectation/conclusion with equivalent wording and certainty, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Confirming the presence or absence of genome editing practices in previous publications is crucial for the effective design and establishment of research using genome editing. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This specific methodological/priority claim does not appear in the publication text and is therefore omitted from the final set of claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: multiDGD is a scalable deep generative model that provides a probabilistic framework to learn shared representations of transcriptome and chromatin accessibility. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: multiDGD is a scalable deep generative model that provides a probabilistic framework to learn shared representations of transcriptome and chromatin accessibility. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution claim intact, with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: multiDGD demonstrates outstanding performance on data reconstruction without feature selection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: multiDGD demonstrates outstanding performance on data reconstruction without feature selection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim appears unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: multiDGD learns well-clustered joint representations on several data sets from human and mouse. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: multiDGD learns well-clustered joint representations on several data sets from human and mouse. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this empirical result with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Probabilistic modelling of sample covariates enables post-hoc data integration without the need for fine-tuning. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Probabilistic modeling of sample covariates enables post-hoc data integration without the need for fine-tuning. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conceptual conclusion about covariate modeling and post-hoc integration is maintained in the publication with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: multiDGD can detect statistical associations between genes and regulatory regions conditioned on the learned representations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: multiDGD can detect statistical associations between genes and regulatory regions conditioned on the learned representations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this capability claim verbatim, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TCRpeg outperforms state-of-the-art methods in estimating the probability distribution of a TCR repertoire, boosting the accuracy from 0.672 to 0.906 measured by the Pearson correlation coefficient. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The average accuracy of TCRpeg in estimating TCR repertoire probability distribution is boosted from 0.672 to 0.906 as measured by the Pearson correlation coefficient. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides the same numerical performance improvement and metric, so the claim is effectively unchanged and directly matched to the specific numeric result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TCRpeg improves on a range of TCR-related tasks, including revealing TCR repertoire-level discrepancies and classifying antigen-specific TCRs. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TCRpeg improves on a range of TCR-related tasks, including profiling TCR repertoire probabilistically and classifying antigen-specific TCRs. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same set of improved tasks (profiling/classifying), with only minor wording differences; the substance is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TCRpeg provides a novel approach to decipher complex immunogenomic repertoires. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TCRpeg provides a novel approach for deciphering complex immunogenomic repertoires. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim about providing a novel approach is carried over into the publication with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepCOLOR is a computational framework that recovers inter-cellular colocalization networks with single cell resolution. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The missing information recovery approach fills in missing sequence and structure information needed to complete proteins.
alignments.pub_claim_text: 'The inpainting' method fills in additional sequence and structure to create a viable protein scaffold in a single forward pass through a specifically trained RoseTTAFold network.
alignments.rationale: The publication gives the same functional description but specifies the implementation (inpainting via RoseTTAFold and single forward pass), which clarifies rather than changes the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The two approaches, constrained hallucination and missing information recovery, demonstrate considerable synergy in protein design.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state or emphasize a claim of synergy between the two methods; no directly corresponding publication claim was found.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AlphaFold2 structure prediction calculations indicate that the approaches can accurately generate proteins with a wide array of functional sites.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Candidate immunogens, receptor traps, metalloproteins, enzymes, and protein-binding proteins were designed and validated using in silico and experimental tests.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents concrete design and validation across many protein types (and experimental tests), which supports and strengthens the preprint's AF2-based accuracy claim though it uses different specifics and broader validation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CITRUS predicts patient-specific transcription factor activities and reveals transcriptional program variations between and within tumor types.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CITRUS predicts patient-specific transcription factor activities and reveals transcriptional program variations between and within tumor types.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this claim verbatim with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CITRUS yields biological insights into delineating transcription factors associated with somatic alterations in individual tumors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CITRUS yields biological insights into delineating transcription factors associated with somatic alterations in individual tumors.
alignments.rationale: The claim is reproduced identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CITRUS is a promising tool for precision oncology.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CITRUS is a promising tool for precision oncology.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same evaluative conclusion about CITRUS.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dissipative systems evolve in the preferred temporal direction indicated by the thermodynamic arrow of time.
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on empirical neural-time-asymmetry findings and does not make the broader theoretical claim about dissipative systems and the thermodynamic arrow of time.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Brain activity time series recorded during conscious wakefulness could be distinguished from their inverted counterparts with high accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Brain activity time series recorded during conscious wakefulness could be distinguished from their inverted counterparts with high accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same empirical result with equivalent framing and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Classification accuracy was reduced for data acquired during deep sleep and under ketamine-induced anesthesia.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Classification accuracy was reduced for data acquired during deep sleep and under ketamine-induced anesthesia.
alignments.rationale: This specific empirical observation is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Transitions between slow (≈20 Hz) and fast frequencies (> 40 Hz) are the main contributors to the temporal asymmetry observed during conscious wakefulness.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Transitions between slow (approximately 20 Hz) and fast frequencies (>40 Hz) were highlighted as the main contributors to the temporal asymmetry observed during conscious wakefulness.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same finding but with higher stated certainty, thereby amplifying confidence while preserving the claim content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A preferred temporal direction is simultaneously manifest in the neural activity evoked by conscious mentation and in the phenomenology of the passage of time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A preferred temporal direction is manifest in the neural activity evoked by conscious mentation and in the phenomenology of the passage of time.
alignments.rationale: The publication states the same conclusion more definitively (higher certainty); this clarifies and strengthens the original formulation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: InDeep is a tool for predicting functional binding sites within proteins that could host protein epitopes or future drugs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: InDeep is a tool for predicting functional binding sites within proteins that could host protein epitopes or future drugs.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The benchmark of InDeep demonstrates that our tool outperforms state of the art ligandable binding sites predictors when assessing PPI targets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The benchmark of InDeep demonstrates that it outperforms state-of-the-art ligandable binding sites predictors when assessing PPI targets and conventional targets.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the benchmarking claim to include conventional targets as well as PPI targets, broadening the scope while preserving the core performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: InDeep offers new opportunities to assist drug design projects on PPIs by identifying pertinent binding pockets at or in the vicinity of PPI interfaces.
alignments.pub_claim_text: InDeep offers new opportunities to assist drug design projects on PPIs by identifying pertinent binding pockets at or in the vicinity of PPI interfaces.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this conclusion without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeLUCS matches true taxonomic groups for large and diverse datasets, with accuracies ranging from 77% to 100%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeLUCS matches true taxonomic groups for large and diverse datasets, with accuracies ranging from 77% to 100%.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this empirical performance claim identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeLUCS significantly outperforms two classic clustering methods (K-means++ and Gaussian Mixture Models) for unlabelled data, by as much as 47%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeLUCS significantly outperforms two classic clustering methods (K-means++ and Gaussian Mixture Models) for unlabelled data, by as much as 47%.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same comparative performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeLUCS is able to cluster datasets of unlabelled primary DNA sequences totalling over 1 billion bp of data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeLUCS is able to cluster datasets of unlabelled primary DNA sequences totalling over 1 billion bp of data.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this scalability claim as stated.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeLUCS bypasses common limitations to classification resulting from the lack of sequence homology, variation in sequence length, and the absence or instability of sequence annotations and taxonomic identifiers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeLUCS bypasses common limitations to classification resulting from the lack of sequence homology, variation in sequence length, and the absence or instability of sequence annotations and taxonomic identifiers.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains this conclusion about DeLUCS's ability to circumvent typical classification limitations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SMURF learns MSAs that mildly improve contact prediction on a diverse set of protein and RNA families. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SMURF learns multiple sequence alignments (MSAs) that mildly improve contact prediction on a diverse set of protein and RNA families. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content and result are the same between preprint and publication; the publication lowers the reported certainty from high to medium.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: By connecting our differentiable alignment module to AlphaFold and maximizing predicted confidence, we can learn MSAs that improve structure predictions over the initial MSAs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: By connecting the differentiable alignment module to AlphaFold2 and maximizing predicted confidence, we can learn MSAs that improve structure predictions over the initial MSAs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is effectively identical (minor naming update to AlphaFold2) and maintains the same level of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The alignments that improve AlphaFold predictions are self-inconsistent and can be viewed as adversarial. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The alignments that improve AlphaFold predictions are self-inconsistent and can be viewed as adversarial. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is restated verbatim in the publication with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work highlights the potential of differentiable dynamic programming to improve neural network pipelines that rely on an alignment. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work highlights the potential of differentiable dynamic programming to improve neural network pipelines that rely on an alignment. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is preserved identically between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are potential dangers of relying on black-box methods for optimizing predictions of protein sequences. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include this limitation about dangers of black-box optimization; the claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Functional-Hybrid model demonstrates successful implementation for four benchmark systems and a microbial fermentation reactor. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Functional-Hybrid model demonstrates successful implementation and interpretability in modeling chemical reactions and other biological processes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the reported implementations and explicitly emphasizes interpretability, broadening the stated application beyond the original specific systems.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the Functional-Hybrid model is at least two times better in interpolation and extrapolation compared to a similar implementation with the conventional ANN. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: During interpolation, the Functional-Hybrid model performs similarly to the Hybrid-ANN model but offers greater interpretability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The strong quantitative claim (>=2x better across interpolation and extrapolation) was weakened and limited to interpolation context where performance is reported as similar; the claim was therefore revised and narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework can learn the dynamics in 50% lower number of experiments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Functional-Hybrid model outperforms the Hybrid-ANN model when data is scarce, making it more suitable for scenarios with a low number of experiments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication removes the specific '50% lower' quantitative claim and rephrases the result as a more general advantage in low-data scenarios, clarifying and narrowing the original numeric statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The improved performance of the Functional-Hybrid model can be attributed to the structure imposed by the functional transformations introduced. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The superior extrapolation capabilities of the Functional-Hybrid model compared to the Hybrid-ANN model can be attributed to the structure imposed by the functional transformations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The attribution to the model structure is retained but is linked specifically to extrapolation performance in the publication, and certainty is increased.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There were no significant differences in the prediction performance between the physics-based model and the neural network model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There were no significant differences in the prediction performance between the physics-based model and the neural network model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The primary result comparing prediction performance is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network model does not require an understanding of the underlying principles of cycling or measurements of fixed parameters such as system weight or wheel size. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural network model does not require an understanding of the underlying principles of cycling or measurements of fixed parameters, providing a practical advantage. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the practical advantage but reduces certainty (high to medium) and streamlines wording, clarifying the claim while tempering confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The physics-based model offers the advantage of interpretability, which is important for scientists seeking to understand the process being modeled. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The physics-based model offers interpretability, which is important for scientists seeking to understand the modeled process. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The interpretability advantage of the physics-based model is preserved with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Both models demonstrated good prediction accuracy despite using fewer input variables than traditional models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This publication-level claim about both models achieving good accuracy using fewer inputs does not appear in the preprint and therefore is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our study generates thousands of AGO2 target sites verified by chimeric reads. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our study generates thousands of AGO2 target sites verified by chimeric reads. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our novel analysis pipeline reveals thousands of AGO2 target sites driven by microRNAs and a significant number of AGO2 ‘drivers’ derived from fragments of other small RNAs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our novel analysis pipeline reveals thousands of AGO2 target sites driven by microRNAs and a significant number of AGO2 ‘guides’ derived from fragments of other small RNAs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication corrects/changes the term 'drivers' to 'guides' but maintains the same substantive result and certainty; this is a terminology clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We utilize convolutional neural networks to train machine learning models that accurately predict the binding potential for each ‘driver’ class. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We utilize convolutional neural networks to train machine learning models that accurately predict the binding potential for each ‘guide’ class. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same method and claim but the publication replaces 'driver' with 'guide', a terminological clarification while preserving meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our comprehensive analysis of the AGO2 targetome broadens our understanding of its ‘driver’ repertoire and potential function in development and disease. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our comprehensive analysis of the AGO2 targetome broadens our understanding of its ‘guide’ repertoire and potential function in development and disease. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is preserved but the terminology is changed from 'driver' to 'guide', a clarification in wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We offer practical bioinformatic tools for future experiments and the prediction of AGO2 targets. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We offer practical bioinformatic tools for future experiments and the prediction of AGO2 targets. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim about tools is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EIFP-4,096 performed (R² = 0.61) superior to related molecular and interaction fingerprints. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EIFP-4,096 performed superior to related molecular and interaction fingerprints with an R² of 0.61. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance result, including the R² value, is the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LUNA supports interpretable machine learning models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LUNA supports interpretable machine learning models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The interpretability claim about LUNA is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Interaction fingerprints can accurately identify similarities across molecular complexes that other fingerprints overlook. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Interaction fingerprints can accurately identify similarities across molecular complexes that other fingerprints overlook. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about interaction fingerprints is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LUNA and its interface fingerprints are promising methods for machine learning-based virtual screening campaigns. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LUNA and its interface fingerprints are promising methods for machine learning-based virtual screening campaigns. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim about LUNA's promise for virtual screening is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CATCH model forecasts fish stock probability densities over time and space in Icelandic waters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CATCH model forecasts fish stock probability densities over time and space in Icelandic waters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result verbatim, with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model demonstrates high accuracy, low error metrics, and strong structural similarity to observed data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Syrjala's test yielded nonsignificant P-values, indicating that the predicted and observed distributions are statistically indistinguishable. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a specific statistical test supporting the preprint's claim of structural similarity; however the reported certainty is lower (medium) so the claim is presented more cautiously.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CATCH generalizes well across key species such as Atlantic cod, haddock, saithe, golden redfish, and Greenland halibut. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieves favourable performance metrics for cod and other target species, indicating its effectiveness. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both describe effective performance across target species; the publication frames this as favourable performance metrics consistent with the preprint's generalization claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results suggest that deep learning models have the potential to optimize fisheries operations and enhance sustainability. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results suggest that deep learning models have the potential to optimize fisheries operations and enhance sustainability. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is the same, but the publication states it with higher certainty (medium vs low), so the confidence is amplified.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model utilizes large-scale Icelandic fishing fleet data integrating multidimensional inputs to produce accurate forecasts. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a methodological/contribution claim about data integration that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The shift towards subcutaneous administration of biologics therapeutics improves patient compliance compared to traditional intravenous infusions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state or reiterate this conclusion about subcutaneous administration improving compliance, so the claim appears removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High viscosity in administered solutions can increase injection time and pain at the injection site. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: High viscosity in biologic therapeutics increases injection time and causes pain at the injection site. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High viscosity formulations can prolong residence time at the injection site, affecting absorption kinetics and potentially altering therapeutic efficacy. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: High viscosity formulations can prolong residence time at the injection site, affecting absorption kinetics and potentially altering therapeutic efficacy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content is the same but the publication presents it with higher certainty (high vs medium), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A multimodal feature learning workflow can predict the viscosity of antibodies by integrating multiple data sources. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A multimodal feature learning workflow can predict the viscosity of antibodies in therapeutics discovery. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed viscosity prediction approach integrates multiple data sources, including sequence, structural, and physicochemical properties. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds specifics about the types of data integrated (sequence, structural, phys-chem) that the preprint did not enumerate.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides insights on the intrinsic viscosity predictive potential of various viscosity prediction methods in therapeutics antibody early development pipelines. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study provides insights into the intrinsic viscosity predictive potential and usability of various viscosity prediction methods in early-stage therapeutics antibody development. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is restated with the same focus and certainty on insights into predictive potential and usability in early-stage development.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Perseverance model was the best model and predicted a gradual reduction in amplitudes of reward prediction errors (RPEs) upon void magazine nosepokes (MNPs). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The perseverance model was the best model, predicting a gradual reduction in amplitudes of reward prediction errors (RPEs) upon void magazine nosepokes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same model selection and prediction result with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of the Perseverance model was verified by the acute intraperitoneal injection of low-dose methamphetamine (METH), which increased the frequency of MNPs during the PR session without changing the breakpoint. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Acute intraperitoneal injection of low-dose methamphetamine increased the frequency of magazine nosepokes during the PR session without changing the breakpoint. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental verification with methamphetamine and its behavioral effect is reported the same way in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings suggest that the Perseverance model reveals effects of psychoactive drugs on choice behaviors during PR tasks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The perseverance model reveals the effects of psychoactive drugs on choice behaviors during PR tasks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The PR task has a limitation in that it only provides a single data point, known as the breakpoint, which does not capture variations in choice behavior. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report this limitation statement about the PR task, so the claim appears omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Fiber photometry confirmed the prediction of dopamine dynamics in the ventral striatum of male mice. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a new experimental confirmation (fiber photometry) that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The perseverance model captured behavioral modulation as a result of increased initial action values, which are typically disregarded in reinforcement learning analysis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces an additional interpretive conclusion about initial action values altering behavior that was not in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High stakes slow down responding, especially in motivationally incongruent conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: High stakes slow down responding, especially in motivationally incongruent conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The main result about slowing under high stakes is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High stakes do not affect whether a response is made or not, and do not change the magnitude of Pavlovian biases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: High stakes do not affect whether a response is made or not, and do not change the magnitude of Pavlovian biases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this negative result with no change in scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Response slowing under high stakes might reflect heightened cognitive control that is ineffectively used. (certainty=medium, type=speculation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Response slowing under high stakes might reflect heightened cognitive control, which is ineffectively used. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The content is the same but the publication frames this interpretation more as a conclusion rather than speculation, slightly amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pavlovian biases and slowing under high stakes may arise in parallel to each other. (certainty=medium, type=speculation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pavlovian biases and slowing under high stakes may arise in parallel to each other. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses this relationship as a conclusion rather than speculation, modestly increasing the claim's assertiveness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: KGML-xDTD is a Knowledge Graph-based Machine Learning framework that predicts treatment probabilities between drugs and diseases while providing biologically explainable mechanisms of action. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: KGML-xDTD is a Knowledge Graph-based Machine Learning framework that predicts treatment probabilities between drugs and diseases while providing biologically explainable mechanisms of action. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this core contribution identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework achieves state-of-the-art performance in both drug repurposing predictions and the recapitulation of human-curated drug mechanisms of action. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework achieves state-of-the-art performance in both drug repurposing predictions and the recapitulation of human-curated drug mechanisms of action. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the stated performance claims with the same wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: KGML-xDTD is the first model framework to offer KG-path explanations for drug repurposing predictions, which can reduce 'black-box' concerns and increase prediction confidence. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: KGML-xDTD is the first model framework to offer knowledge graph path explanations for drug repurposing predictions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework leverages knowledge-and-publication based information to extract biologically meaningful demonstration paths in the drug repurposing process. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework can effectively reduce 'black-box' concerns and increase prediction confidence for drug repurposing. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HADDOCK can generate accurate models of antibody-antigen complexes using an ensemble of antibody structures generated by machine learning tools and AlphaFold2 predicted antigen structures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HADDOCK can generate accurate models of antibody-antigen complexes using an ensemble of antibody structures generated by machine learning tools and AlphaFold2 predicted antigen structures. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Targeted docking using knowledge of the complementary determining regions on the antibody and some information about the targeted epitope allows the generation of high quality models of the complex with reduced sampling. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Targeted docking using knowledge of the complementary determining regions on the antibody and some information about the targeted epitope allows the generation of high-quality models of the complex with reduced sampling. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording and meaning are the same between preprint and publication; no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computationally cheap protocol developed in this study outperforms the ZDOCK baseline. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The computationally cheap protocol developed outperforms the ZDOCK baseline. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new model for neural codes accurately estimates the likelihood of individual spiking patterns. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model for neural codes accurately estimates the likelihood of individual spiking patterns. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model's performance on simultaneously recorded spiking activity of over 100 neurons is comparable or better than that of current models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model's performance on spiking activity of over 100 neurons is comparable with or better than that of state-of-the-art models. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can be learned using a small number of samples and a local learning rule that utilizes noise intrinsic to neural circuits. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model can be learned using a small number of samples and a local learning rule that utilizes noise intrinsic to neural circuits. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Slower, structural changes in random connectivity improve the efficiency and sparseness of neural representations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Random sparse connectivity is suggested as a key design principle for neuronal computation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Entorhinal and ventromedial prefrontal cortex (vmPFC) representations perform a much broader role in generalising the structure of problems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Entorhinal and ventromedial prefrontal cortex (vmPFC) representations perform a broader role in generalizing the structure of problems. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Entorhinal representations are preserved across different reinforcement learning (RL) problems only if task structure is preserved. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Entorhinal representations are preserved across different reinforcement learning (RL) problems only if task structure is preserved. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Representations of standard RL signals such as prediction error in vmPFC vary as a function of task structure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Representations of prediction error in vmPFC and ventral striatum depend on task structure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the signal of interest to prediction error specifically and extends the anatomical scope to include ventral striatum, altering details of the original claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Humans and other animals can abstract structural knowledge and generalize it to solve new problems. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This higher-level conclusion about abstraction and generalization is present in the publication but was not stated as a claim in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Changes in reward expectations activate ventral striatal areas. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Changes in reward expectations activate ventral striatal areas. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mean reward rates preferentially modulate the substantia nigra/ventral tegmental area, deep layers of the superior colliculi, and a posterior pontomesencephalic region. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mean reward rates preferentially modulate the substantia nigra/ventral tegmental area, deep layers of the superior colliculi, and a posterior pontomesencephalic region. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No substantive changes in content, scope, or confidence are present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Specific regions in the brainstem are recruited regardless of prediction errors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Specific regions in the brainstem are recruited regardless of prediction errors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Brainstem structures may work together to set motivation and attentional effort levels according to perceived reward opportunities. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Brainstem structures may work together to set motivation and attentional effort levels according to perceived reward opportunities. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding interpretation is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Vicarious rewards can drive reinforcement learning in male rats. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mutual reward delivery in male rats can drive associative learning. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core phenomenon with slightly different terminology ("mutual reward delivery" and "associative learning"), clarifying experimental context without a major change in confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The transmission of social cues is necessary for reinforcement learning to occur in the context of vicarious rewards. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The social unblocking effect is absent when social information exchange is impeded. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements express the same necessity of social information transmission; the publication frames it as absence of effect when exchange is blocked, preserving meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When actor rats have fully learned a stimulus-self reward association, adding a cue that predicted additional partner reward unblocked associative learning about this cue. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The social unblocking effect is present when controlling for secondary reinforcement. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the unblocking result but adds that it holds when secondary reinforcement is controlled for, strengthening (amplifying) the claim's evidential support.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Additional cues that did not predict partner reward remained blocked from acquiring associative value. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The social unblocking effect is absent when the added cue predicts reward delivery to an empty chamber. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a specific instance (cue predicting empty chamber) of the more general preprint result that cues not predicting partner reward remain blocked; core meaning is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Preventing social signal exchange between the partners resulted in cues signaling partner reward remaining blocked. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The social unblocking effect is absent when social information exchange is impeded. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This is a direct restatement: preventing social exchange abolishes the unblocking effect, matching the publication claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The social unblocking effect is absent when mutual reward outcomes are disadvantageously unequal to the actor. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes this condition-dependent negative result about unequal reward outcomes which was not present in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The expected free energy functional does not incorporate prior beliefs about outcomes into the generative model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The expected free energy functional does not include prior beliefs about outcomes as part of the generative model. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with very similar wording; the reported certainty is lower in the publication, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generalized free energy functional explicitly includes priors over outcomes as a component of the generative model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generalised free energy functional incorporates priors over outcomes as an explicit component of the generative model. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same result (same content) but with lower reported certainty, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Policies selected using the free energy functional minimize uncertainty about future outcomes by minimizing expected free energy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim explicitly asserts that policy selection minimizes uncertainty about future outcomes by minimizing expected free energy, so this specific claim appears absent in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The form of posterior beliefs about policies is identical under both the expected and generalized free energy formulations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Policies selected under both free energy formulations yield identical posterior beliefs about policies despite differences in the quantities used for computation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core claim that posterior beliefs about policies are identical but adds the clarification that this holds despite computational differences, so the claim is clarified without changing confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ProteinMPNN achieves a sequence recovery of 52.4% on native protein backbones, outperforming Rosetta's 32.9%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ProteinMPNN has a sequence recovery of 52.4% on native protein backbones, compared to 32.9% for Rosetta. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact numerical result and comparison, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporation of noise during training improves sequence recovery on protein structure models and produces sequences that more robustly encode their structures. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims do not mention the training-noise intervention or its effects, so this specific methodological/result claim is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ProteinMPNN demonstrates broad utility and high accuracy in experimental tests, including X-ray crystallography and cryoEM. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate the specific experimental modalities (X-ray, cryoEM) or that set of experimental accuracy claims, so this claim is absent from the listed publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ProteinMPNN successfully rescues previously failed designs made using Rosetta or AlphaFold. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ProteinMPNN demonstrates broad utility and high accuracy in rescuing previously failed designs made using Rosetta or AlphaFold. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the rescue result but reports it with higher certainty and broader emphasis on utility and accuracy, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.pub_claim_text: ProteinMPNN enables coupling of amino acid sequences between single or multiple chains, allowing for a wide range of protein design applications. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This capability is stated in the publication but has no corresponding preprint claim, so it is an added contribution in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Aligned network embeddings ensure consistency across species without sacrificing quality compared to species-specific network embeddings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Aligned network embeddings ensure consistency across species without sacrificing quality compared to species-specific network embeddings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Aligned network embeddings are complementary to sequence embedding techniques. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Aligned network embeddings are complementary to sequence embedding techniques. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim is present verbatim in the publication with the same framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Training logistic regression classifiers on aligned network embeddings and sequence embeddings improved the accuracy over using sequence alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Training logistic regression classifiers on aligned network embeddings and sequence embeddings improved the accuracy over using sequence alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this experimental result directly and with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the combined embeddings reached numbers close to state-of-the-art deep-learning methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the combined embeddings reached numbers close to state-of-the-art deep-learning methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same performance claim with equivalent confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trained models successfully detected the expansion of cell nuclei upon aphidicolin treatment and a decrease in the cell aspect ratio upon propyzamide treatment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The trained models successfully detected the expansion of cell nuclei upon aphidicolin treatment and a decrease in the cell aspect ratio upon propyzamide treatment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this experimental detection result identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Metrics such as cell area, circularity, and solidity obtained from virtual staining analyses were highly correlated with those obtained by manual measurements of cell features from microscopy images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Metrics such as cell area, circularity, and solidity obtained from virtual staining analyses were highly correlated with those obtained by manual measurements of cell features from microscopy images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this correlation result with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The versatility of virtual staining was highlighted by its application to track chloroplast movement in Egeria densa. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The versatility of virtual staining was highlighted by its application to track chloroplast movement in Egeria densa. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this illustrative application and framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method was effective for classifying live and dead BY-2 cells using texture-based machine learning. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method was effective for classifying live and dead BY-2 cells using texture-based machine learning. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this effectiveness result with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The non-invasive nature and efficiency of this method make it highly suitable for label-free, dynamic, and high-throughput analyses in quantitative plant cell biology. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The non-invasive nature and efficiency of this method make it highly suitable for label-free, dynamic, and high-throughput analyses in quantitative plant cell biology. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates this concluding claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Granny is a tool designed for rating fruit using machine-learning and image-processing to address rater bias and improve resolution. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Granny is a tool designed for rating fruit using machine-learning and image-processing to address rater bias and improve resolution. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same contribution claim without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current Granny ratings include starch content assessment, rating levels of peel defects, and peel color analyses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current Granny ratings include starch content assessment, rating levels of peel defects, and peel color analyses. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrative analyses enhanced by Granny’s improved resolution and reduced bias will further enrich our understanding of fruit quality dynamics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrative analyses enhanced by Granny's improved resolution and reduced bias will further enrich our understanding of fruit quality dynamics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this forward-looking conclusion with the same framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Granny supports backwards compatibility by providing ratings compatible with long-established standards and references. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Granny supports backward compatibility by providing ratings compatible with long-established standards and references. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
